Preparation and Clarification of Requirements
Schedule the Meeting :
Arrange a time for you and Rohi to meet to review UI stories.○
Prepare a list of the stories you want to discuss.○•
Review UI Stories :
During the meeting, lead the discussion on identifying:
Missing requirements: Note any ambiguities or lack of information.•
Dependencies: Identify any interdependencies with other stories or components.•
Acceptance criteria: Ensure each story has clear acceptance criteria for success.•••
Specific Tasks for EFI 2639 and 2629 :
Break down the requirements for these specific stories.•
Confirm details with John during or after the meeting if you find anything unclear.••
Create the Epic Template :
Use the Epic template to document UI -related stories. •
Organize them in a structured manner for clarity, ensuring that each story links back to the 
overall objective.••
Plan and Host a UI Breakout Session
Help Define Session Agenda :
Collaborate with Rohi to create a focused agenda for the breakout session.•
Ensure that the agenda covers all critical aspects of the UI requirements and discussions.•
Participate Actively in the Breakout Session :
Facilitate discussions around UX design requirements and ensure all key elements are 
addressed.•
Provide input on IAM integration and authorization specifics based on your analysis.•
Document Key Decisions :
Take notes on decisions made during the session, particularly regarding the 
development of API endpoints and other workflows.••••
Task Breakdown and Jira Updates
Define Tasks for Development :
After the breakout session, work on breaking down stories into specific tasks.•
Assign tasks to developers where appropriate, considering the details discussed.••
Update Jira :
Regularly check and update the Jira board with the latest task statuses.•
Ensure that all UI -related tasks have clear acceptance criteria, functional requirements, and 
technical details.••
Design, Development, and Testing Checkpoints
Schedule Weekly Syncs :
Propose a recurring weekly sync with Rohi and any relevant stakeholders to review 
progress.•
Prepare an agenda for each meeting to ensure discussions stay focused.•
Prepare Test Cases :
Collaborate with the testing team to draft test cases for integration testing and UAT 
based on the UI stories.•
Ensure that test cases cover key workflows and user scenarios.•••
Backlog Reviews and Daily Standups
Engage in Daily Backlog Review Sessions :
Attend  erry’s daily sessions and contribute by providing updates on the UI stories and any 
blockers.•
Use these sessions to align with the team on priorities and ensure that UI tasks are on track.•
Participate in Daily Standups :
Provide updates on your tasks during standups, highlighting any challenges or needs 
for assistance.•
Collaborate with Rohi and other team members to adjust plans based on feedback 
and discussion.•••
Additional Considerations:
Maintain Open Communication :
Regularly communicate with Rohi and the development team to ensure alignment and 
address any issues as they arise.••
Document Everything :
Keep thorough documentation of all discussions, decisions, and changes made throughout 
the process for future reference and transparency.••1. Initial Review and Assessment
Start by Reviewing the Backlog :
Open the backlog and skim through the user stories and tasks.•
Identify which stories are most relevant to your current objectives, especially those related 
to UI and the specific projects like EFI 2639 and 2629.••
2. Identify Key User Stories
Select User Stories to Focus On :
Choose a small number of high -priority or critical user stories that need immediate 
attention. This will help prevent overwhelm.•
Look for stories with unclear requirements, dependencies, or acceptance criteria.••
3. Schedule a Team Meeting
Gather Your Team :
Schedule a meeting with Rohi and any other relevant team members (e.g., developers, UX 
designers) to discuss your findings.••
Meeting Agenda :
Review selected user stories together.•
Identify any gaps in information or clarity.•
Discuss dependencies and whom to reach out to for further information (e.g., UX team).••
4. Clarify Requirements
During the Meeting :
Go through the selected user stories one by one.•
Document any missing requirements, dependencies, or unclear acceptance criteria.•
Note down any points that require reaching out to other teams for clarification (like UX).••
5. Set Clear Action Items
Define Next Steps :
Assign action items to team members for each identified gap (e.g., “Rohi will reach out to 
the UX team about design specifications for story X”).•
Ensure that each action item has a responsible person and a timeline.••
6. Create a Prioritized Action Plan
Organize the Backlog :
Based on the discussions, create a prioritized list of user stories that need to be addressed. 
This will guide your work moving forward.••
Use the Epic Template :
Begin documenting user stories in the Epic template for better organization.••
7. Engage with Stakeholders
Reach Out :
For any user stories requiring input from the UX team or other stakeholders, start initiating 
those conversations.•
Set up meetings or send emails to gather the necessary information.••
8. Start Small
Pick One or Two User Stories :
Once you've clarified requirements and dependencies for a couple of stories, begin breaking 
them down into actionable tasks.•
This will help you build momentum without feeling overwhelmed.••
9. Regular Check -Ins
Establish a Weekly Sync :
Set a recurring meeting to review progress on the selected user stories and tasks.•
Use this time to identify any blockers and adjust your plans as needed.••Hi John,

corelate to this layer. Every template can only have a single finance version. 
Copacker plants will have a template for production. We need to tie exclusivity to the finance. They 
cannot create any other bom type if the finance bom doesn’t exist. Only applicable for non -copacker 
plants
For co -packer plants they do not create finance versions at all they create production versions.

Each template will be unique combination of cost center and material, if same material is defined it 
must have different cost center.

Our business partners gave data core rules for zcwp's given a plant inventory point will always be

Participants: Shannon, Andre, Global Source Team
Objective: Define business requirements and functional specifications.
Key Points Discussed:
Business Requirements Definition: 
Collaborating with Global Source to define business requirements.•
Estimated time: 5+ hours to define all functional specifications.•
Shannon and Andre will validate the requirements.•
Post -validation, they will finalize the business requirements and functional specifications.•1.
BOM -Data Facts: 
Document: BOM -Data Facts.xlsx •2.
Cost Center –BOM Template: 
Format: 8 -digit (First 4 digits: plant reference, Last 4 digits: cost code). •
Data Source: Master data reference call center from GCP.•
Question: Do users know which cost center to use? We will populate the cost center table.•3.
BOM Templates: 
Each BOM template can only have a single finance version.•
Copacker plants will have a template for production and must tie exclusivity to the finance BOM.•
Non-copacker plants cannot create any other BOM type if the finance BOM doesn’t exist.•
Co-packer plants do not create finance versions; they create production versions.•
Each BOM template will be a unique combination of cost center and material. If the same 
material is defined, it must have a different cost center.•
No multiple finance versions allowed.•
Standards: Define what will work best for us when creating versions. Engineering will be 
documented manually.•4.
Managing Standards: 
Discussion around managing standards and ensuring consistency.•5.
Formulation Suspend and Resume Points: 
Discussion on how to handle formulation suspend and resume points.•6.
Inventory Points (ZCWP's): 
Defined 4 types of ZCWP's; none will create an inventory point.•
Core rules for ZCWP's provided by business partners.•
Inventory point will always be introduced at the same step within the formulation composition, 
regardless of production location.•
A formulation will have a ZCWP for the same step no matter where it is produced.•
Consideration: Would it make sense to demonstrate that this freeze step is an intermediate 
BOM template?•7.
Follow -Up Questions:
Cost Center Usage: 
How will we ensure users know which cost center to use?•
What is the process for populating the cost center table?•1.
BOM Templates: 
Can we clarify the process for creating finance and production versions for both copacker and 
non-copacker plants?•
What are the specific steps to ensure each BOM template is unique in terms of cost center and 
material?•2.
Managing Standards: 
What specific standards need to be documented in the engineering manual?•
How will we ensure consistency in managing these standards?•3.
Formulation Suspend and Resume Points: 
What are the criteria for defining suspend and resume points in formulations?•4.
Inventory Points (ZCWP's): 
Can we get more details on the 4 types of ZCWP's?•
How will we ensure that the ZCWP is introduced at the same step within the formulation 
composition across different production locations?•
Would demonstrating the freeze step as an intermediate BOM template be beneficial? If so, 
how?•5.7/9/2025

UI Rough Drafts Backlog in Jira
Issue ID Description
EFI-2639 UI Rough Drafts for BOM Assembly
PHASE2 Attach UI Rough Drafts
Link issue
ALM Test Coverage
New Action:
As a BOM business stakeholder or BOM UI developer, I have a document presenting a clear vision for 
the functionality surfaced in the BOM Assembly UI, so that I have a tangible artifact demonstrating the 
application goals and can participate in the approval and development of the UI.
Acceptance Criteria
Application UI wireframes/mocks and workflow document, with approval by:
UI developer for feasibility•
Mid-tier developer for feasibility•
UX designer for usability and application standards•
Business Analyst and Business for functional requirements••
Technical Details
The application UI vision has a critical relationship to the core application data schema, each informing 
the other; both should be drafted concurrently.

EFI-3107 1.
2
PHASE2 -Okta Configuration for BOM Portal
Link issues, web pages, and more
In Progress
Actions
Description
As an admin, I need to configure Okta for BOM Portal so that users can authenticate and authorize access 
to new UIs like Formulation Viewer.
Acceptance Criteria
Ensure the following is accomplished for DEV, QA, PREP, PROD environments: 
Custom Authentication Server set up for BOM Portal•
Okta application is configured correctly for BOM Portal.•
Appropriate scopes and claims are set up in Okta.•
Okta groups and roles match BOM Portal permissions.•
Documentation for Okta configuration is created and shared with the team•
Technical Details
OKTA ARCHITECT: Paco Cisneros
Lower Okta Access Environments:
https://login -dev.tyson.com/enduser/settings
https://login -qa.tyson.com/enduser/settings
Set up Okta Custom Authentication Server for BOM Portal. •
Set up Okta application and configure appropriate settings for BOM Portal.•
Ensure necessary scopes and claims are included in the Okta configuration.•
Create and manage Okta groups and roles that correspond to BOM Portal permissions.•

PHASE2 -Structured SysLog: Splunk Dashboard
Link issues, web pages, and more
New
Actions
Description
Develop a baseline Splunk dashboard that categorizes external and defect errors, summarizes endpoint 
request quantity and response times, displays application restarts, and counts exception types, and provide 
short team Splunk training and demo.
Acceptance Criteria
defects and integrated / external system failures are clearly distinguished and surfaced in a published 
enterprise log dashboard•
validation errors are visible with a unique Id linking to object that failed validation•
missing configurations are visible including which deployment and setting is missing•
application restarts are visible•
average API response times by endpoint are visible•
uncategorized logs are observed in order to have a plan of action to address in a more meaningful manner•
team members demonstrate basic Splunk query familiarity•
Technical Details

EFI-3026 1.
1
PHASE2 -Validation User Logs
Link issues, web pages, and more
New
Actions
Description
As a BOM Portal user, I can understand the concise/non -technical validation summary, so that I am 
not frustrated by resulting rejections during human -initiated actions such as BOM creation.
Acceptance Criteria
Validation summary has the following qualities:
easily understandable by target audience•
distinguishes problems requiring external action, internal business action, and internal defect 
resolution•
provides sufficient information to initiate resolution•
captures failures for new formulations (with no replica present yet)•
differentiates violation of PDM validation vs. BOM -extended validations •
Technical Details
The validation error result pattern must include not only data that is systematically consumable, but 
also information that is easily transformed to a human -presentable response. This data is collected, 
most likely in the database store to represent relation to formulations and materials.
EFI-3028 1.
1
PHASE2 -Formulation Event/Status UI
Link issues, web pages, and more
New
Actions
Description
As a BOM Portal user, I can see formulation status and event history in a category -searchable UI, so 
that I can understand changes, resolve business effects from poor statuses, and escalate if necessary.
Acceptance Criteria
Formulation Viewer is enhanced to provide a unified view of all formulation events
Supports viewing formulation without resolving expiry•
Permits search by last updated date•
Permits search by last -known inconsistent •
Updates succeeded including version•
Updates failed due to validation•
Updates failed due to external system failures•
Updates failed due to defects•
Update entries include cause of update•
Displays failures for new formulations (with no replica present yet)•
Late -discovered Inconsistencies, e.g. from periodic or demand scans•
Dependent BOM navigation links•
   UXUI Feature Page 555    
Dependent BOM navigation links•
Technical Details
When validation errors arise, log to persistence that can be summarized or searched in a BOM Portal 
UI. This is probably captured to the main BOM Portal MySQL database. It will depend on a UI 
implementation; because this is a formulation -centric issue, the formulation -viewer may be the 
appropriate hub to present this information. A formulation with a current SpecChange error status 
should be observable while viewing the formulation (or any BOM derived from it in the future).
EFI-3027 1.
1
PHASE2 -Formulation Event Audit
Link issues, web pages, and more
New
Actions
Description
As a BOM Portal maintainer, I can see audit history of all successful and failed BOM Portal 
formulation update events, so that I can trace the origin of current data and avoid production risks.
SaveCancel
Acceptance Criteria
Formulation updates are entirely contained within a logged failure check•
The log schema supports records of late -discovered inconsistencies •
Technical Details
Formulation updates can fail for reasons other than validation failures, such as integrated system 
failures and software defects. All failures, whether observed or discovered later by dependent 
processes, must be captured for presentation in a unified report. Changes to formulations and 
affected BOMs are recorded in an audit log in a manner than can be presented in a unified report 
with Validation User Logs and other formulation events.
Add parent

Description

Acceptance Criteria
Application UI wireframes/mocks and workflow document, with approval by:
   UXUI Feature Page 556    
Application UI wireframes/mocks and workflow document, with approval by:
UI developer for feasibility•
Mid-tier developer for feasability•
UX designer for usability and application standards•
Business Analyst and Business for functional requirements•
Technical Details
The application UI vision has a critical relationship to the core application data schema, each 
informing the other; both should be drafted in concurrently.

PHASE2 -Inconsistent Formulation Summary
Link issues, web pages, and more
New
Actions
Description
As a Cost Accounting member, I can view formulations and related BOMs that are not current, so that 
I can avoid production risks.
Acceptance Criteria
All rejected updates have human -readable causes identified •
Includes periodic reconciliation information•
Includes eventual consistency digest comparisons or comparable evaluations•
Technical Details
This should be summarized in the formulation status report; however, it will likely also include special 
knowledge provided by the eventual consistency implementation and a periodic formulation 
reconcilation process.

EFI-3030 1.
1
PHASE2 -Microversion Replacement
Link issues, web pages, and more
New
Actions
Description
As a BOM business data owner, I have clear and concise control over what fields constitute a BOM -
relevant formulation change, so that I can make BOM workflow decisions based on my business 
needs.
Acceptance Criteria
Formulation updates that have no effect on BOMs can be distinguished to avoid requiring BOM 
Publishing of those updates•
Technical Details
Implement a formulation property digest or other property -comparison -based strategy that identifies 
whether formulation changes are relevant to BOM outcomes, and permits tracking the drift of 
dependent BOM Versions relative to those changes.
EFI-3033 1.
1
PHASE2 -Formulation Version Check
Link issues, web pages, and more
New
Actions
Description
As a consumer of PDM OfficialProduct, I can uniquely identify a material formulation update with 
confidence by a concise value, so that I may quickly verify data for a specific material.
Acceptance Criteria
A summary technique is implemented with sufficient confidence to rapidly verify that a single 
formulation is current•
Optionally provides a targeted consistency guarantee, e.g. via a digest.•
Technical Details
Today, BOM Portal identifies SpecChanges by version ID. Because this value is manually adjusted, 
human errors can result in multiple formulation variations with a single version; and ignoring the 
latest variation. We must have a concise value that is guaranteed to uniquely identify the formulation 
values that BOM Portal consumes; less optimially this may identify formulation changes that are 
irrelevant to BOM Portal.
EFI-3034 1.
1
PHASE2 -Formulation Reconciliation
Link issues, web pages, and more
   UXUI Feature Page 558    
New
Actions
Description
As a BOM business data owner, I have a backup system to verify every formulation is consistent, so 
that I have the confidence to transition away from legacy recipe -based recon to single -instance 
formulations.
Acceptance Criteria
Inconsistent formulations are periodically discovered, logged, and autocorrected•
The periodic process runs with frequency sufficient to offset any reliability shortcoming of the 
eventual consistency strategy and human error•
Technical Details
These results must be aggregated to formulation event UI.

As part of our efforts to ensure the BOM Portal is designed to best support your work, we are

potential enhancements that could make the system more efficient and user -friendly. This 
feedback will be pivotal as we collaborate with the UI/UX team to refine the design and 
functionality of the portal.

this by 11/8:

that you find particularly helpful or frustrating?•

limitations?•1.

   UXUI Feature Page 565    
-> Probably need to have secondary sessions with plant maintainers and field users.
1. Overview of Survey Responses The survey conducted aimed to gather detailed insights from key 
stakeholders, including Ann Parker, Geof Schroeder, Doug Wolfe, and Angelica Flores, to pinpoint 
current limitations and potential enhancements for the BOM Portal. Their feedback underscored the 
need for improvements in UI/UX design, functionality, and technical capabilities to create a more 
efficient, user -friendly, and stable system.
2. Key Themes Identified
A. Design -Related Enhancements (UI/UX Improvements)
UI Navigation Challenges : Stakeholders expressed frustration with the excessive number of clicks 
needed for various tasks and frequent page timeouts that disrupt workflow. A clearer and more 
streamlined navigation layout was identified as essential for improving user efficiency.•
Direct Code Management : Users noted the inability to check or uncheck codes directly within 
individual BOMs, necessitating navigation to parent BOMs to make these changes.•
Processing Aid Section : A dedicated section for managing processing aids was suggested, enabling 
manual adjustments without altering the main recipe formulation.•
Visual Elements : While users appreciate the “recipe used by” option showing connected codes, 
the current design is considered inefficient for navigating specific tabs and sections.•
Advanced Disassembly Suggestions : A feature that recommends disassembly codes based on pre -
existing BOMs from other locations would greatly streamline processes.•
B. Functional Enhancements
Mass Operations : The current process for terminating and approving BOMs requires individual 
handling, making it labor -intensive. Stakeholders emphasized the need for mass approval and 
termination functionalities.•
Change Tracking System : A comprehensive change log for monitoring and reviewing modifications 
is lacking, which complicates tracking and troubleshooting.•
Material and Resource Syncing : The existing material and resource syncing functionality has 
ceased working. Restoring and enhancing this feature is critical for data consistency.•
ECM Process Issues : The ECM process is currently unreliable due to database structure problems. 
Enhancing this process will stabilize it, ensuring it can be used effectively without requiring 
manual MRP runs.•
Template -Based BOM Management : A proposed template -based approach would minimize the 
need to create redundant spec copies for multiple BOM versions.•
AOP BOM Management : The ability to maintain a separate AOP (Annual Operating Plan) BOM 
instance that can be converted to a  inance BOM at the fiscal cycle’s end is a desired feature.•
C. Technical System Enhancements
Okta Integration and IAM Features : Stakeholders highlighted the need for Okta integration for 
secure user authentication and role -based access management (IAM) to bolster system security.•
System Integration Considerations : Ensuring that system upgrades align with existing 
integrations, such as SAP and the Engineering Standards Portal, is crucial for seamless data flow 
and functionality.•
Retention of Current Capabilities : Stakeholders stressed that while enhancements are necessary, 
the BOM Portal must retain its current capabilities, including:
Support for multiple BOM types (Finance, Production, Engineering, AOP).•
Customizable structures such as unchecked inventory points and alternate production ••RG -Survey Response Summary
Wednesday, November 13, 2024 2:57 PM
   UXUI Feature Page 566    
Customizable structures such as unchecked inventory points and alternate production 
BOMs.•
Assigning rework materials and moving packaging between steps, including manual 
packaging usage.•
Comprehensive "What -If" BOM functionality for cost analysis. •
Continued support for existing admin functions and current usage and scrap percentage 
calculations.•
3. Key Frustrations and Limitations
Timeouts and Refresh Issues : Frequent session timeouts necessitate page refreshes, which 
disrupts workflow and efficiency.•
Spec Change Propagation : The portal does not effectively propagate specification changes to 
plant inventory points not linked to corporate specs.•
Approval and Termination Workflow : The workflow for approving spec changes in bulk is 
cumbersome, requiring entry into each BOM individually. Terminating BOMs is also labor -
intensive due to the need to disconnect attached codes and remove versions in steps.•
4. Suggested Enhancements and ‘Nice -to-Have’ Features
Processing Aid Management Section : Stakeholders proposed a feature allowing processing aids to 
be adjusted without impacting the core recipe.•
Enhanced Disassembly Functionality : Suggested improvements include the ability to recommend 
disassembly codes based on pre -existing setups.•
Direct Code Management : Implementing direct code management within individual BOMs was 
highlighted as a user -friendly enhancement.•
5. Desired Reports and Real -Time Updates
Alerts for Key Status Changes : Notifications for status changes such as flagged deletions, B4 or Y3 
status updates, and procurement type shifts from E to F were recommended.•
Disconnect Alerts : Real -time updates for BOM -spec disconnects were deemed essential for 
effective monitoring.•
Conclusion The survey responses highlight an imperative for a balanced approach that integrates UI/UX 
design improvements, functional workflow enhancements, and technical system upgrades. By 
addressing these key areas, the BOM Portal will achieve enhanced performance, usability, stability, and 
security while retaining its current core functionalities.
Functional Scope for Enhancements and Stability Improvements
Maintain Support for Multiple BOM Types : The system must continue supporting Finance, 
Production, Engineering, and AOP BOM types.•
Customizable BOM Structures : Ensure continued support for unchecked inventory points, 
alternate production BOMs, and plant inventory points.•
Enhanced Assignments : Retain capabilities for rework materials, alternate materials, and global 
scrap percentages while enabling direct adjustments for processing aids.•
System Integration : Align new enhancements with current integrations such as SAP and the 
Engineering Standards Portal for seamless functionality.•
Security and User Access : Implement Okta integration for enhanced security and IAM for role -
based access.•
Admin and Disassembly Functions : Ensure retention of existing admin capabilities and robust 
disassembly functionality to meet current and future user needs.•
These changes will address user pain points, create operational efficiencies, and ensure the BOM Portal 
remains a vital tool for stakeholders.

Mass Termination Capability for BOMs8.
Description: Implement a system that allows users to perform mass termination of all types of BOMs, 
simplifying the workflow and reducing manual effort.Description
The goal of this project is to enhance the database structure and code base to alleviate issues with system 
instability and to promote a more robust and useable program.
Business Case
Our current BOM Portal instance never met the requirements set forth by project Philadelphia. Since go 
live, we lost our developers, and any support or enhancement was taken over by Infosys. Since BOM 
Portal was an in -house developed program, this transition never fully occurred, and we have not been 
successful.
Our current instance of BOM Portal has stability issues and the database structure lends itself to 
corruption. This is dealt with daily by our support team. Our first step toward correcting BOM Portal is 
the formulation master implementation. Scheduled for September 10, this change will bring in a master 
copy of the product spec. This master copy will be used to update BOM’s within BOM Portal. This will 
make the spec change process more efficient and should help alleviate system issues with spec changes. 
This project will update how BOM’s are treated by BOM Portal. Currently a separate copy of the spec is 
maintained for each BOM instance. This is cumbersome and leads to issues with spec changes and with 
BOM creation (cloning). The new approach is to maintain a “master BOM” at the plant level for each 
production process. Any BOM’s needed for the location would use one of these “master BOM’s” as a 
template whereby attributes would be assigned. This will keep us from bogging the database down with 
needless replication of spec data and should facilitate the following. All of these were requirements for 
the original program within the Philadelphia project.
1. Unchecked Inventory Points –Our current instance will create these; however, the database/code 
structure does not handle them well. The current logic creates a “Recipe Group” in Bom portal, this is 
something that needs to be addressed. Once a spec is unchecked it should no longer create a recipe group, 
but it should still be able to receive spec changes. These are the largest cause of our issues. They are 
necessary as we do not want to create plant specific specs or even multiple specs for a location based on 
the process flow.
2. ECM Process –Our current ECM process is broken because of our database construction. This change 
will allow us to use this functionality. Especially for Production BOM’s. Currently we are not using 
ECMs for production BOM because of constant breaking of BOM’s. This forces MRP to be run off -line 
manually.
3. AOP BOM –Ability to keep a separate BOM instance to use for the annual operating plan and convert 
it to the Finance BOM at the end of the planning season before the next fiscal year.
In attrition to the enhancements given BOM Portal should retain current functionality while enhancing 
performance, stability, useability, upgradability, and security.
BOM Portal needs to retain current functionality. Examples are given below (non -inclusive list).
1. Support of multiple BOM Types. (Finance, Production, Engineering, AOP)
2. Support of multiple process options. (Batch VS Continuous)
3. Ability to customize BOM structure (Unchecked Inventory Points, Alternate Production BOMs, Plant 
Inventory Points)
4. Ability to assign rework materials.
5. Ability to move packaging between steps within a spec.
6. Ability to manually set usage on packaging. (This should be held upon spec refresh)
7. Ability to select item type and units of measure for components.
8. Interface with and receive data from our Engineering Standards Portal.
9. Ability to assign step yields.
10. Ability to select alternate materials.
11. Ability to attach phantom materials.
12. Ability to have both item and global scrap percentages.
13. Ability to assign network and resource to a BOM.
14. Ability to manually assign throughput and crew to a BOM.
15. Ability to use demonstrated data for throughput and crew.
16. Ability to use Engineering Change Management.
17. Ability to terminate a BOM without deleting it in SAP.
18. Ability to show plant instructions; corp instructions; corp usage and standards (Text Tab)
19. Retain current calculations for usage.
20. Ability to create What IF BOM’s for cost analysis.
21. Retain all current Disassembly functions.
22. Retain all Admin functions currently present.
The largest benefit will be a functional and useful BOM Portal system. BOM Portal is the backbone of 
our production and cost process for Tyson. Every product produced at a Poultry or Prepared plant had its 
BOM/Recipe created in BOM Portal and interfaced to SAP. BOM portal ensures consistent calculations 
and consistent delivery of data to SAP. BOM Portal is also the system which houses our Engineered 
Standards and delivers these standards to SAP. BOM Portal, via the not -on-BOM check, is the first line of 
defense against an incorrect material being issued to an order. Without BOM Portal in place, Tyson would 
have to employ 70 or more team members to manually manage BOM’s for each location in SAP. This 
would strip us of another of BOM Portal’s advantages, which is consistency in BOM creation. The largest 
asset brought to us by BOM Portal is adherence to the product specification.
Technical Case
The enhancements to BOM Portal are necessary to prolong its service life, correct outdated interfaces, 
and enhance performance thus alleviating errors.
Our current code is not documented. A recently identified error resulted in 160 -man hours of work by 
experienced contract developers to find the root cause. This is not a sustainable situation. The 
enhancements will have clearly documented architecture and maintainable code written to current 
industry standards.
Currently BOM Portal does not have logging. Implementation of logging will enhance our ability to 
determine the root cause of issues.
Our current interfaces are using outdated non -standard protocols (SingalR). These interfaces have no 
external contracts and cannot be consumed by non -Microsoft systems or user interfaces. Upgrade to
these interfaces is necessary to ensure we are receiving all spec changes and submit to SAP quickly and 
reliably.
Our current Instance of BOM Portal does not use Tyson standard security protocols. This leaves Tyson’s 
data vulnerable which is not acceptable for a SOX controlled system.
Currently Tyson has no on staff experts in BOM Portal from a development standpoint. Normalizing the 
code along with proper documentation will aid in training Tyson staff developers to support BOM Portal.Requirements
Tuesday, November 12, 2024 9:27 AM

Description: Implement a system that allows users to perform mass termination of all types of BOMs, 
simplifying the workflow and reducing manual effort.
Acceptance Criteria:

The UI supports batch termination actions with clear feedback.•
Technical Details:
Document user requirements for mass termination, ensuring alignment with business needs.•
Implement backend logic for mass termination and update the UI.•
Design user -friendly UI elements for batch termination, ensuring minimal impact on system 
resources.•
Feedback Alignment:
Simplifies the termination process and reduces manual effort, aligning with user requests to 
streamline workflows.•
Addresses the current limitation of having to disconnect attached codes and delete versions one 
at a time.•

Description: Develop a feature that enables users to approve multiple "what -if" scenarios at once to 
streamline decision -making.
Acceptance Criteria:
Users can select and approve multiple "what -ifs" simultaneously. •
The system processes mass approvals efficiently and without performance issues.•
Technical Details:
Outline requirements for mass approval and ensure user expectations are documented.•
Implement batch processing logic for "what -if" approvals, maintaining system stability. •
Create mockups that streamline the mass approval process to enhance user interaction.•
Feedback Alignment:
Saves time and improves efficiency, directly addressing user frustrations with having to approve 
each "what -if" individually.•
Reduces repetitive workflows and manual approval processes.•

navigate to parent BOMs.

Users can check or uncheck codes directly in BOMs.•
Changes are reflected in real -time within the UI. •

Empowers users to manage codes more efficiently, addressing the frustration of having to 
navigate to parent BOMs.•

Here you go!
Current Tasks and Workflows: Understanding your day -to-day use of the BOM Portal
What are the primary tasks you perform on the BOM Portal daily?
Creating new BOMs1.
Deleting obsolete BOMs2.
Updating existing BOMs3.○
Can you describe any workflows that you find repetitive or time -consuming?
Deleting BOMs from BOM Portal. To be able to terminate BOMs in BOM Portal, 
which would delete BOMs and recipes from SAP as well, we need to disconnect 
all codes attached to the BOM we are terminating, and we have to delete all 
versions one at a time.1.
Approving what -ifs. We have to approve what -ifs one at a time because the 
mass process doesn’t currently work.2.
Deleting BOMs from BOM Portal after they’ve been terminated. After 
terminating a BOM, we have to refresh the screen, go to the effective dates 
tab, and click on the “x” next to the date to completely remove the BOM from 
BOM Portal.3.
Approving spec changes. We can approve in mass but have to go into each 
BOM to see what the spec change is.4.○
Are there specific design aspects or user interface elements within the BOM Portal 
that you find particularly helpful or frustrating?
HELPFUL:
Being able to see the codes connected with the “recipe used by” option.1.1.
FRUSTRATING:
Having to refresh the page every 5 -8 minutes because it times out. 1.
Not being able to terminate phantoms, APBs, or plant inventory points.2.
Not being able to remove or add plant inventory points on future ECMs 
without affecting the current BOM.3.
Not being able to receive spec changes on plant inventory points not 
connected directly to a corporate spec.4.
BOMs going from published to edit status for non -material changes. 5.
Not being able to check or uncheck a code from its own BOM, but instead 
having to go to the parent BOM.6.2.○
What tasks do you frequently need to perform outside of the BOM Portal due to its 
limitations?
Deleting BOMs and recipes from SAP through a help desk ticket for plant 
inventory points, phantoms, and APBs.1.
Researching BOMs changes in BOM Portal because BOM Portal currently 
doesn’t record changes.2.
Syncing materials and resources. This feature stopped working.3.○1.
Key Metrics and Dashboard Insights: Identifying the data and KPIs that matter most
Which metrics or KPIs are the most essential for you to monitor on your dashboard?○2.

   UXUI Feature Page 573    
Design
Main Content: Home Page 
Navigation Bar :
Located at the top, it includes options like Home , Search/Create , Audits , Admin , Disassembly , 
Reports , Material Request , SiteAdmin , and Dashboard . •
Home button takes you to home page•
Search/Create button opens a new window allows for easy searching based on Plant ID and Recipe 
ID. If a recipe is not found, a dropdown is shown to create •
The audits button takes us to the recipe audit page•
Sidebar :
On the left, there’s a sidebar showing a list of production sites, each labeled with their 
corresponding plant names and their associated plant IDs. Each site has a count indicator next to 
it, possibly representing the total number of materials within each location.•
The sidebar also includes filter categories like Total , Spec Changes , In Edit , and In Error , with 
corresponding counts for easy tracking.•
Main Content Area :
This section is split into two main sections:
Header Section : Displays the selected plant with a dropdown for selecting BOM type 
(Production, Finance, etc)  and summary counts (e.g., Total, Edit).○
Table Section : Displays a table of recipes/materials with columns such as Material , Type , 
Version , Start Date , End Date , Status , Published Date , Update Date , Update User , Create 
Date , and Create User . This layout supports detailed tracking of each material or recipe.○
Action Buttons :
There are controls for filtering/searching, adjusting the page size (items per page), 
columns (ability to select which columns show), Action (once a material is selected the 
following actions can take place: 
Send to SAP -Likely sends the current data or specification to the SAP system. •
Send to SAP /w Phantom -Sends data to SAP with additional parameters or 
configurations labeled as "Phantom."•
Send to SAP /w Debug -Sends data to SAP with debugging enabled, possibly for 
testing or troubleshooting purposes.•
Delete -Removes the selected data, record, or item. •
Clone Finance -Creates a duplicate of the finance -related data or configuration. •
Clone Engineering -Creates a duplicate of the engineering -related data or 
configuration.•
Clone AOP -Clones data or configurations related to the Annual Operating Plan 
(AOP).•
Approve Spec -Approves a specification, marking it as reviewed and accepted. •
Move to Edit -Changes the status of the specification or item to an editable 
state.•
Refresh Spec -Updates or reloads the specification with the latest information. •
Load Esp Standards -Loads or applies ESP (presumably Environmental, Social, 
and Governance standards) to the data.•
Create ECMs What If -Generates hypothetical "What If" Engineering Change 
Management (ECM) scenarios.•
Test What If -Tests the hypothetical ECM scenarios to see possible outcomes or 
impacts.•
Approve What If -Approves the "What If" scenario after testing, possibly 
making it official or moving it forward for implementation.•▪a.•
Main Content: Recipe Audit
https://bomportal -dev.cloud.tyson.com/Audit/#
Filters:
Plant Dropdown: Allows users to search audits by a specific plant and material •
Material Dropdown: Provides the option to select a specific material associated with a recipe. •
Search Box: Users can search audits by entering keywords or criteria. •
Table Columns: The table below the filters is designed to display audit information
Version: represents the version of the recipe or BOM. •
BOM Type: Specifies the type of BOM associated with the recipe. •
Recipe ID: Unique identifier for each recipe. •
Audit Date: Shows when the audit was conducted. (Column sorted in ascending or 
descending order as indicated by the arrow.)•
User Name: Identifies the user who performed or initiated the audit. •
Audit Status: Displays the status of the audit (e.g., completed, pending). •
Recipe Status: Indicates the current status of the recipe (e.g., active, inactive, in progress). •••
Admin dropdown: Recipe Admin
https://bomportal -dev.cloud.tyson.com/RecipeAdmin/#/
Main Functional Area

users to select a plant location for filtering materials.•

down the search.•
Buttons :

material number.•

current data.•

Admin dropdown: User Security
https://bomportal -dev.cloud.tyson.com/UserSecurity/
A prominent search bar at the top allows for quick searching of users by name, username, There is also 
a button to the right of the search bar to create a bom Portal User•
Table Layout:
User information is presented in a clean and organized table format, enhancing readability and 
ease of use.•
Table Columns:
User Security Access Icon/Button: This button when selected opens a page displaying a 
user's access to the different BOM's as well as all the functionalities ▪
User Plant Access Icon/Button: This button when selected opens a page that has a table 
of plants that the specific user is subscribed to. There is a checkbox list of all plants and an 
apply and close button at the bottom ▪
Action Icon: Next to the User Plant Access icon, there is a button that allows to activate or 
deactivate users. User's that are active already show an X by their name to deactivate, 
users that are not active show a check by their name to activate the user. ▪
Email: Should Display User's email, however only displays Username ▪
Full Name: Shows the user's full name, making it easy to identify individual users. ▪
Last Viewed: Indicates the last time the user accessed the system, helping administrators 
track user activity.▪••

   UXUI Feature Page 577    
User Story 1: Okta Sign -In Integration •
As a BOM Portal user, I want to securely log in using Okta so that I can ensure my access is 
authenticated and secure.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : Enhancing security through a reliable authentication system reduces risks associated 
with unauthorized access.•
Technical Considerations :
Integration with existing IAM (Identity and Access Management) protocols.○
Compatibility with multi -factor authentication (MFA) for added security. ○•
Dependencies : Implementation of Okta API, coordination with IT security teams for integration. •
Acceptance Criteria :
Users can successfully log in using Okta credentials.○
Role -based access is enforced based on the user’s profile after login.○•
User Story 2: User Role and Permission Management•
As an admin, I want to manage user roles and permissions in the BOM Portal so that I can control 
access based on user roles and responsibilities.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : Role -based access control ensures that only authorized personnel can perform 
specific actions, maintaining data integrity and security.•
Functional Requirements :
Admins should be able to create new roles, assign permissions, and delete or modify 
existing roles.○
Detailed audit trails should track changes made to user roles.○•
Dependencies : Integration with Okta’s IAM capabilities, security policy adherence. •
Acceptance Criteria :
Admins have a dashboard to manage user roles effectively.○
Role modifications trigger notifications to system administrators.○•
User Story 3: Enhanced UI Navigation•
As a BOM Portal user, I want the UI to be more intuitive with fewer navigation steps so that I can 
access essential features quickly and efficiently.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : Improved navigation minimizes user frustration and increases productivity. •
User Experience (UX) Considerations :
Redesign key workflows for fewer clicks.○
Implement consistent menu structures and shortcuts for frequent tasks.○•
Technical Requirements :
Use modern front -end frameworks to enhance responsiveness. ○•
Acceptance Criteria :
Users experience a 30% reduction in the number of clicks needed for common tasks.○
User feedback on new UI designs is positive, with approval ratings above 80%.○•
User Story 4: Mass Termination Capability•
As a BOM Portal user, I want to terminate multiple BOMs at once so that I can save time and 
effort in managing obsolete data.•User Stories
Wednesday, November 13, 2024 2:18 PM
   UXUI Feature Page 578    
effort in managing obsolete data.
Expanded Details : •
Justification : Mass termination improves efficiency and minimizes repetitive manual work. •
Functional Requirements :
Users should be able to select BOMs through a multi -select feature and initiate batch 
termination.○
Termination actions should have an “Undo” option for a limited time to prevent errors.○•
Performance Considerations :
Ensure batch operations do not impact overall system performance or lead to timeouts.○•
Acceptance Criteria :
Users can terminate multiple BOMs simultaneously without performance lags.○
Batch terminations are logged with details for audit purposes.○•
User Story 5: Mass Approval of "What -If" Scenarios •
As a BOM Portal user, I want to approve multiple "what -if" scenarios at once so that I can 
streamline my decision -making process.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : This feature reduces the time needed to handle bulk data approvals and supports 
efficient scenario planning.•
Functional Requirements :
Users should be able to filter and select "what -if" scenarios based on defined criteria (e.g., 
date, status).○•
Risk Management :
Ensure batch approvals do not create conflicts in concurrent data changes.○•
Acceptance Criteria :
Approvals are processed efficiently, with notifications for completed actions.○
Approval process includes validation checks to ensure data integrity.○•
User Story 6: Comprehensive Change Log•
As a BOM Portal user, I want to view a detailed change log of BOM modifications so that I can 
track changes and troubleshoot issues.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : A comprehensive change log provides transparency and aids in compliance and 
troubleshooting.•
Technical Requirements :
The log should display who made changes, what changes were made, and timestamps.○
Filtering options for date ranges, user activity, and type of change.○•
Data Storage Considerations :
Ensure that change log data is stored securely and efficiently.○•
Acceptance Criteria :
Users can access and filter the change log by multiple criteria.○
The log is updated in real -time with minimal latency. ○•
User Story 7: Material and Resource Syncing•
As a BOM Portal user, I want material and resource syncing to work reliably so that I can maintain 
data consistency across the system.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : Data consistency is crucial for accurate reporting and workflow management. •
Functional Requirements :
Implement real -time syncing and status checks for updates to materials and resources. ○
Include automatic conflict resolution where feasible.○•
   UXUI Feature Page 579    
Include automatic conflict resolution where feasible.○
Performance Considerations :
Ensure that syncing operations do not affect portal performance during peak usage times.○•
Acceptance Criteria :

User Story 8: Alert System for Key Status Changes•
As a BOM Portal user, I want to receive alerts for significant status changes so that I can act 
promptly when necessary.•

Justification : Alerts enable timely responses and proactive management. •

User Story 9: Enhanced ECM Process•
As a BOM Portal user, I want ECM updates to reflect consistently in the BOM so that I can ensure 
data stability and avoid production issues.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : Consistent ECM updates minimize disruptions in production planning. •
Technical Requirements :
Integrate ECM changes into the portal with real -time data validation. ○
Implement automated alerts for discrepancies detected during ECM updates.○•
Acceptance Criteria :
ECM changes are accurately updated and reflected in related BOMs.○
No data discrepancies occur post -update. ○•
User Story 10: Dedicated Processing Aid Management•
As a BOM Portal user, I want a dedicated section for managing processing aids so that I can adjust 
them without impacting the main formulation.•
Expanded Details : •
Justification : This feature supports flexibility in managing processing aids without altering primary 
recipes.•
Functional Requirements :
A user -friendly interface for managing processing aid quantities. ○
Controls for tracking adjustments without affecting overall formulation balance.○•
Acceptance Criteria :
Users can make adjustments in a dedicated section without impacting the core formulation.○
Audit trails capture all changes made to processing aids for review.○•

EFI-3033PHASE2 -
Formulation Version 
CheckAs a consumer of PDM 
OfficialProduct, I can uniquely 
identify a material 
formulation update with 
confidence by a concise value, 
so that I may quickly verify 
data for a specific material.A summary technique is implemented with sufficient confidence to 
rapidly verify that a single formulation is current•
Optionally provides a targeted consistency guarantee, e.g. via a 
digest.•Today, BOM Portal identifies SpecChanges by version ID. Because this value is 
manually adjusted, human errors can result in multiple formulation variations with 
a single version; and ignoring the latest variation. We must have a concise value 
that is guaranteed to uniquely identify the formulation values that BOM Portal 
consumes; less optimially this may identify formulation changes that are irrelevant 
to BOM Portal.4

PDM is SAAS Product Data Management
Restricted to internally that’s why SAAS 
PDM is composed on different modules
General Specification management
GSM accessible through interface we have on pdm
Pdm -dev.tyson.com/portal
Ledger on what has been officialized to be 
Trade specification search 
Follows a certain order to rigorously verify how it works
Table with different statuses, all different formulation versions
Spec # and status for that material, Spec name material
Trade units have all of the formulation data and references
Like the ledger with hyperlinks in terms of processing, terms, and components. 
PDM needs to ensure only official values are sent to bom portal, 
We need officiail in matterial master data in both SAP and 
Both trade in and consumer unit
Trade unit is above the consumer unit in the hierarchy
The formula is atached to the consumer unit. 
Formula is the process and how the material is going to be built, the sequence of the recipe. 
This is everythign we want to obtain in PDM some of the
We want to see this for our official product payload is like 7k lines the condensed version is 200. : 
(condensed version)
Bom is based out of formulation
Formulation is standardized way to produce bill of materials 
The recipe is the formulation because it tells you how much egg, yolk, whatever your using and whole 
qty of yields which makes a certain quality of hotcakes 
Boms enter between the hotcakes and the bake. 
Boms are batches of production that need to be inspected on a costing level. 
A bom must always contain a formulation. 
Recipe is operation proccess like the steps 
A bom is the costing of the goods that go into creation of formulation. How much does each cost how 
much do I use?
Recipe is just extraction of process
Recipe must contain one step. 
Importance of having pdm always in sync with Bill of Materials.

1. Project Overview
This project aims to implement various enhancements to the BOM Portal and Dashboard, focusing on 
improved security, user experience, workflow efficiency, change tracking, and data management.
2. Project Objectives
Integrate secure Okta sign -in and identity access management. •
Implement mass operation capabilities for BOMs.•
Enhance the user interface for better navigation.•
Introduce change -tracking features and alert notifications. •
Restore material and resource syncing functionality.•
Improve the Engineering Change Management (ECM) process.•
Add a dedicated section for processing aid management.•
3. Scope of Work
In-Scope :
Okta sign -in integration. •
User role and permission management.•
Mass termination and approval functionality.•
UI navigation improvements.•
Comprehensive change log implementation.•
Alert system for key status changes.•
Material and resource syncing restoration.•
ECM process improvements.•
Dedicated processing aid management section.••
Out-of-Scope :
Major overhauls of unrelated modules.•
Non -BOM -specific enhancements. ••

Performance Testing : Validates that the system performs optimally under load. •

   UXUI Feature Page 604    
Thursday deadline. 
EOD Thursday 
Engagement with Business Team for Requirements Gathering
Objective : To understand the day -to-day workflows, processes, and specific needs of end -users 
(e.g., Doug in development, Angelica in field side plant control, and production line staff).•
Actions :
Schedule two 1 -hour sessions with key stakeholders. •
Prepare targeted questions about current workflows and expectations from the new 
system, especially in relation to the BOM portal.•
Ask hypothetical questions, like “If the BOM portal didn’t exist, what would you ideally want 
it to accomplish?” to gather forward -thinking insights.••
2. Collaborate with UX Team
Objective : To ensure user -centered design practices align with the business’s expectations and 
technical requirements.•
Actions :
Review the Statement of Work (SOW) for any preliminary insights on UX deliverables and 
engagement.•
Set up office hours (Tuesdays and Thursdays) for regular interaction and support.•
Organize a “walk -a-mile” session where IT members shadow business users to experience 
their workflows firsthand.•
Verify UX team needs from the SOW and establish the extent of their engagement in 
development activities.••
3. Assess Blazor as a Technology Choice
Objective : Understand and prepare for potential challenges associated with implementing Blazor for the 
first time, especially given the team's limited experience with it.•
Pros of Blazor :
.NET Integration : Native to .NET, which could streamline integration with other .NET -based 
systems.•
Single Language Development : Enables C# for both front -end and back -end, reducing the need to 
juggle multiple languages.•
Microsoft Support : Provides dedicated Microsoft support, which may simplify troubleshooting 
and long -term maintenance.•
Cons Compared to React/Angular :
Learning Curve : Limited team experience and industry knowledge base could delay delivery. •
Community and Resources : While Blazor is open -source, it has a smaller community and 
resource pool than more established technologies like React or Angular.•
Dependence on Microsoft Ecosystem : Tied more closely to Microsoft tools, which may limit 
flexibility.•
Next Steps :
Discuss with Rohi, who has Angular experience, whether it would be beneficial for the 
team to start prioritizing learning Blazor.•
Engage Zach, who has developed the formulation viewer in Blazor, to offer insights or 
conduct knowledge -sharing sessions.•
Consider assigning team members to start with small Blazor tasks to build fluency.•••
4. Development and Testing Roadmap
Objective : To ensure a smooth development process with clear roles, responsibilities, and 
expectations.•
Actions :
Confirm that the UX team can actively engage in development if specified in the SOW.•
Define a collaborative plan with Microsoft’s development team, particularly in areas 
where Microsoft IPs are integrated or if specialized Blazor knowledge is required.•
Plan for ongoing alignment with UX and development teams to ensure the project 
stays on track with deliverables and quality expectations.••
5. Schedule an Onsite Meeting and Continuous Follow -up
Objective : Maintain ongoing collaboration and address any emerging issues in real -time. •
Actions :
Schedule an onsite meeting for the following week if the SOW is completed and 
shared with the UX team.•
Regularly check the progress and adjust schedules as needed.•••
Schedule Sessions
Identify and invite stakeholders for the business requirements and UX design sessions.•
Propose dates and times for the sessions, ensuring all key participants can attend.•
Prepare agenda
Draft an agenda for each session, outlining key discussion points such as:•
High -level goals for the BOM Portal•
User needs and expectations•
Key metrics for success•
Gather Preliminary Data
Collect existing documentation related to BOM Portal requirements.•
Review user feedback and insights from previous versions or related projects.•
Conduct Sessions
Facilitate the scheduled sessions, ensuring a collaborative environment for discussion.•
Use visual aids (e.g., whiteboards, sticky notes, digital tools) to capture ideas and feedback in real time.•
Document Requirements
Record business requirements and user expectations discussed during the sessions.•
Ensure all stakeholders have the opportunity to contribute to the documentation.•
Review and Revise
Compile the documented requirements and send them to participants for review.•
Allow time for stakeholders to provide feedback and make revisions as necessary.•
Obtain Approval
Finalize the business requirements document.•
Schedule a follow -up meeting to present the final document and obtain formal approval from 
stakeholders.•
Distribute Final Document
Share the approved business requirements and user expectations document with all relevant team 
members.•
Store the document in a shared location for future reference.•
Follow -Up Actions
Identify any further actions or projects based on the approved requirements.•
Schedule future check -ins to ensure alignment as development progresses. •Agenda for the First Session
Introductions
Briefly introduce all participants and their roles. (Greg -Business Analyst; Rohi –Developer) •
Establish ground rules for communication and collaboration.•
Objectives of the Session
Define the purpose of the session: to gather insights on high -level goals and user needs for the 
BOM Portal.•
Outline expected outcomes, such as documenting initial requirements and understanding user 
expectations.•
Overview of the BOM Portal
Present a brief overview of the current state of the BOM Portal (if applicable) and its purpose.•
Discuss any known issues or areas for improvement based on previous feedback.•
High -Level Goals
Facilitate a discussion on the overarching goals for the BOM Portal. Consider questions like:
What are the primary objectives of the BOM Portal?○
What business problems does the portal aim to solve?○
How will success be measured?○•
User Personas
Identify and discuss the various user personas that will interact with the BOM Portal. For each 
persona, consider:
Roles and responsibilities○
Typical tasks or workflows they will engage in○
Challenges they face in their current processes○•
User Needs and Expectations
Gather input on specific user needs and expectations for the BOM Portal. Discuss:
What features are essential for users?○
Are there any critical functionalities that must be included?○
What are users’ pain points with existing tools or processes?○•
Prioritization of Requirements
Begin a preliminary discussion on the prioritization of the identified needs and expectations. Use 
techniques such as:
Voting on features○
Discussing which needs align best with the high -level goals ○•
Feedback Mechanisms
Discuss how feedback will be gathered throughout the project, including:
Future sessions○
Surveys or user testing○•
Next Steps
Outline the next steps after this session, including:
Follow -up sessions to refine requirements ○
Timelines for documentation and approval○•
Q&A and Open Discussion
Allow time for participants to ask questions and provide additional thoughts or insights.•
Encourage open dialogue to ensure all voices are heard.•
Conclusion
Summarize the key points discussed and clarify any outstanding items.•
Thank participants for their contributions and emphasize the importance of their input in shaping 
the BOM Portal.•Have a session with rohi to gether insights on high -level goals and user needs for bom portal
Outline user expectations. 
Current state of bom portal
Step -by-Step Guide to Begin the First Session
Set a Collaborative Tone
Start with a brief icebreaker to help participants feel comfortable sharing their thoughts and ideas.•
Emphasize that this is a brainstorming session and that all input is valuable.•
Define the Purpose of the BOM Portal
Ask the group to define what they believe the BOM Portal should achieve. Prompt with 
questions like:
What problems does this portal need to solve?•
Why is it important to have this portal for our organization?••
Collect initial thoughts without filtering or critiquing ideas —focus on gathering insights. •
Identify Stakeholders and User Groups
Discuss who the primary users of the BOM Portal will be. Consider various roles and 
departments that may interact with it.•
Create a list of stakeholders who will have input or influence over the portal's 
development.•
Gather Preliminary Input
Use a whiteboard or a collaborative digital tool (like Miro or Google Jamboard) 
to collect input from participants on:
Key features they think the portal should have•
Challenges they currently face in their workflows that the portal could 
address•
Discuss Current Challenges
Facilitate a discussion on existing processes and tools. Ask questions 
like:
What works well in your current processes?•
What are the major pain points or bottlenecks?••
Identify common themes or issues that participants raise.•
Introduce High -Level Goals
After gathering input, introduce some initial high -level goals 
for the BOM Portal based on the discussions. For example:
Improve data accuracy and accessibility•
Streamline workflows for BOM management•
Enhance collaboration between departments•
Create User Personas (if applicable)
If time allows, start sketching out user personas 
based on the stakeholders discussed. Ask 
participants to describe:
What tasks these users will perform•
What information they need•
Any specific requirements they might have•
Outline Next Steps
Summarize the key points discussed 
and identify the next steps:
Schedule follow -up sessions to 
refine requirements based on 
this initial discussion.•
Assign tasks to team members 
to further research specific 
areas or gather additional input 
from other stakeholders.•
Collect Feedback and Close
Encourage participants to 
provide feedback on the 
session format and 
content. Ask them what 
other information or 
resources would be 
helpful for future 
discussions.•
Thank everyone for their 
participation and 
emphasize that their 
contributions are 
essential to the project's 
success.•1.•1.•1.•1.1.•1.1.1.1.
Here's an expanded version of each point, providing more context and prompting stakeholders to think 
in detail about their needs and goals.
User Needs
To better understand how you use the BOM Portal, we would like to discuss the specific tasks and 
workflows that are most important to your role. This will help us identify key features to prioritize. Some 
guiding questions include:
Primary Tasks : What are the main tasks you use the BOM Portal for? Are there repetitive tasks 
that could be streamlined or automated?•
Current Workflows : How do you currently interact with the BOM Portal throughout your •EFI-1001: Assembly Business Requirements and UX Design 
Sessions
Wednesday, October 30, 2024 2:44 PM
   UXUI Feature Page 605    
Current Workflows : How do you currently interact with the BOM Portal throughout your 
workflow? Are there particular steps that are time -consuming, difficult, or redundant?•
Pain Points : Are there any specific challenges or frustrations you experience when using the BOM 
Portal? (e.g., navigation issues, lack of necessary data, slow load times, etc.)•
Improvements : What are some enhancements you believe could improve your daily operations 
within the BOM Portal? Are there features you feel are missing or underutilized?•
By identifying these needs, we aim to create a more intuitive interface that saves time and reduces 
friction.
Key Metrics
To ensure the dashboard provides valuable insights, we’d like to understand which metrics or KPIs are 
most critical for your work. Please consider:
High -Value Metrics : What specific data points or metrics do you frequently monitor? For instance, 
do you track BOM version counts, edit frequencies, error tracking, or completion times?•
Dashboard Capabilities : Would you find it beneficial to have certain data visualized in graphs, 
charts, or tables? Are there key metrics that need to be updated in real -time?•
Alerting and Notifications : Are there any metrics where alerts or notifications would be valuable? •
Historical Data and Trends : Is tracking historical data to observe trends over time something you 
need? If so, which trends would be most useful for you?•
The insights from this discussion will guide us in designing a dashboard that is both informative and 
actionable, supporting your ability to make data -driven decisions.
Strategic Goals
We aim to align the BOM Portal and dashboard improvements with our larger strategic goals to ensure 
they add measurable value to your operations. To that end:
Operational Efficiency : Are there specific ways the BOM Portal and dashboard could enhance 
efficiency or simplify processes? For example, would better search functionality, faster data 
retrieval, or more customization options support your work?•
Supporting Business Objectives : How can the portal and dashboard be designed to help you 
achieve key business objectives? Are there specific targets or outcomes (e.g., reducing error rates, 
increasing data accuracy) that you would like this project to address?•
Growth and Scalability : Are there any anticipated changes in your department or industry that 
may affect your use of the BOM Portal? For instance, are there plans to increase the number of 
users, expand data sources, or integrate with additional systems?•
 our answers will help us create a solution that not only meets today’s needs but also scales with future 
growth and aligns with our overarching strategic vision.
Meeting Details
We are planning to schedule a 60 -minute session to discuss these points in detail. Here are the 
proposed options for date and time:
Option 1 : [Insert Date and Time] •
Option 2 : [Insert Date and Time] •
Location : [Location/Meeting Link]
Please confirm which option works best for you, or let us know if there’s a more convenient time.  our 
feedback in this session will be vital to guiding our project goals, and we greatly value your input.
This expanded outline gives stakeholders specific topics to consider, which will help generate more 
targeted feedback during the meeting.
   UXUI Feature Page 606    
Preparation for Call with Rohi, Andre, Ben, and UX Team
1. Overview of the BOM Portal
Purpose: Clearly articulate the primary goals and functions of the BOM Portal. •
Current Version: Be ready to provide a demo or screenshots of the existing UI. •
2. Usage Details
User Groups: Identify the main users and stakeholders of the portal. •
Usage Scenarios: Prepare examples of how the portal is currently used in workflows. •
Key Features: Highlight existing features that are working well and any pain points. •
3. Feedback and Changes
Anticipate Questions: Be ready to discuss potential changes or improvements to the UI based on 
user feedback.•
Documentation: Have any relevant documentation or user feedback ready to share with the UX 
team.•
4. Involve Andre
His Role: Outline why having Andre on the call will be beneficial (e.g., his insights into the 
technical aspects, user requirements, etc.).•
5. Prepare Questions for UX Team
What specific aspects of the app do they want to focus on?•
Are there any initial thoughts on the current UI and areas for improvement?•
What information or resources do they need from us to proceed?•
6. Logistics
Schedule: Confirm the date and time of the call. •
Platform: Decide on the communication platform (Zoom, Teams, etc.) and share the invite with all 
participants.•

team and get some introduction conversations going. let me know what you think works best!

I hope you’re doing well today! As we prepare for our calls with the UX team, I wanted to touch base to 
ensure we’re on the same page. Were you able to connect with  ohn and get any of your questions 
answered?
I think it would be helpful to discuss key points like the BOM Portal’s purpose, current usage, and any 
questions we anticipate from the UX team. Additionally, having Andre on the call would be beneficial, as 
he can provide a more thorough overview of the BOM Portal's current design. Ben said he would also 
like to be on the call with the UX team. 
Do you have any specific areas you’d like to address?
Would you prefer to set up a call to go over this beforehand, or should we dive right into the discussion 
with the UX team? Let me know what you think works best!Initiate a Friendly Introduction : Reach out informally to the UX team via email or in person. Introduce 
myself, explain my role in the project, and express excitement about working with them. I’d share that, 
even without a formal SOW, I’m keen to align with their expertise to improve the application’s usability.•
Present the Project's Goals and Context : Provide an overview of the application, including high -level 
objectives, target users, and any specific challenges or design pain points. This helps UX understand the 
context and how their input could enhance the user experience, even in the early stages.•
Ask for High -Level Input : Request a brainstorming or informal discovery session to gather initial UX 
perspectives. During this meeting, I’d invite them to share feedback or design considerations based on 
what they know and any prior projects they’ve done. This gives them a sense of involvement without 
formal requirements.•
Share Data and Insights : If available, present data such as user feedback, analytics, or any relevant 
research. Highlighting any current issues users face can help the UX team start conceptualizing ways to 
improve the experience, even without detailed project documentation.•
Prioritize User Flows and Pain Points : Identify specific user flows or challenges that could benefit from 
UX guidance.  or example, “Our users struggle with navigation here” or “This workflow could use 
improvement to boost conversion rates.” Specific questions give UX something concrete to focus on, 
allowing them to contribute meaningfully.•
Request Prototypes or Low -Fidelity Mockups : Since there’s no SOW, I’d avoid asking for polished 
designs. Instead, I’d see if they could provide sketches, low -fidelity wireframes, or ideas that illustrate 
potential solutions, making it clear that initial concepts are sufficient.•
Discuss Potential Next Steps and Future Collaboration : Mention that this engagement can serve as a 
foundation for formalizing UX’s involvement once the project gains further clarity. If possible, offer to 
advocate for an SOW or more structured involvement on their behalf.•
Establish Regular Check -Ins: To keep things moving, I’d set up periodic check -ins to discuss user 
feedback or development updates. This helps UX stay in the loop and continue contributing insights that 
align with our evolving goals.•Action Items

Friday, April 18, 2025 11:00 AM
   UXUI Feature Page 621    
User Testing & Feature Feedback Summary
General UX/UI Improvements
Columns should be autosized, resizable, and support resorting (drag and drop to rearrange or 
remove).•
Enable predictive search functionality that does not require pressing Enter. •
Update search bar label from “Search Material” to just “Search.”•
Forever scroll to be implemented.•
Clicking a BOM should open in a new tab automatically.•
Option to export Excel from the homepage.•
Filtered typing on the plant dropdown field.•
Set default plant to auto -populate on load. •
Allow multi -select functionality for WIP steps with spotlighting capability. •
Material & BOM Settings
Change "BOM Settings" to "BOM Standard" on the Formulation page.•
Effective Date / Expiration Date fields should support future -dated BOMs and ECM dates. •
Add option for Scrap on Packaging.•
Use color coding for material types:
Pink = Inactive/Deleted Material○
Yellow = Edited Material○
Dark Blue = Primary Material○
Light Blue = Alternate Material○•
Color coordination for BOM types and alternates on the Formulation page under yields/settings.•
Inventory & Phantom BOMs
Rename “Inventory” to “Inventory Points.”•
Clarify handling of Phantom BOMs —submission behavior and data tracking. •
Define how inventory points, APBs, and phantoms are handled —note: “The Step is an inventory 
point but does not generate inventory.”•
Confirm whether Engineering BOMs are being phased out.•
Clarify whether WIP only submissions will still be valid moving forward •
Versioning & History
Version History should reflect logical versioning (001, 002, 003, 3031…).•
For Spec Changes, use format like: 7/4/20 (spec 1) or Spec 2.1 . •
Status History should show who did what (action traceability).•
Navigation & Layout
Show all plants on one page if possible.•
Hide material overview by default; let users unhide as needed on formulation page.•User Testing

   UXUI Feature Page 628    
Finance bom dictates how all derived versions will look like and dictates structure of every one. 
Child boms derived from canonical bom type
One to one dependence inbetween template and bom type
Copacker plants finance one –production boms
New bom types to be supported 
Single page to maintain bom template and version 
Emphasis on the need to use single page 
1 canonical bom type per template
Essentially we are enabling users to inact changes on a single page.
Canonical bom cannot be changed, bom on front end can
Button to clone driver button, user should be able to identify which cost center and location they want 
to copy that structure which then creates template on plant, material, cost center. 
Ability to produce similar bom entities with same structure. Cloning also allowed from canonical bom 
level. 
For this we are for sure that for plant configuration and localized materials are succinctly defined. 
If no yellow comments then safe to use. 
All bom versions connected to canonical bom 
Bom template = canonical BOM
Users are not familiar with cost center and numbers, they just want to select plant and resource. Cost 
center
Are they going to choose cost center for canonical bom 
Plant resources have a relationship between cost -center lets just use resource insead of cost center. 
Lets see if we can set a ground right now. 
Resource M:1 Cost Center
Bom versions defined by cost center### Key Points:
1. **Canonical BOM Type**:
   -The canonical BOM dictates the structure and appearance of all derived BOM versions.
   -Child BOMs are derived from the canonical BOM type, maintaining a one -to-one dependency 
between the template and BOM type.
2. **Single Page Management**:
   -Emphasis on using a single page to maintain BOM templates and versions.
   -Users can enact changes on this single page, ensuring streamlined management.
3. **Cloning Capability**:
   -Users can clone BOM structures, specifying the cost center and location.
   -This cloning can occur at the canonical BOM level, allowing for the creation of similar BOM entities 
with the same structure.
4. **Plant Configuration and Localized Materials**:
   -Plant configurations and localized materials are succinctly defined to ensure clarity and accuracy.
5. **Cost Center and Resource Relationship**:
   -Users should select plant and resource instead of cost center, simplifying the process.
   -There is a relationship between plant resources and cost centers, but the focus should be on 
resources.
6. **Safety and Comments**:
   -If there are no yellow comments, the BOM is safe to use.
### Considerations:
1. **User Familiarity**:
   -Users are not familiar with cost centers and numbers; they prefer selecting plants and resources.
   -Simplifying the interface to focus on plant and resource selection can improve user experience.
2. **Resource to Cost Center Mapping**:
   -Establish a clear mapping of resources to cost centers (Resource M:1 Cost Center) to ensure accurate 
BOM versions.
3. **Ground Rules**:
   -Set clear ground rules for the relationship between resources and cost centers to avoid confusion.
### Workflow:
1. **Single Page Management**:
   -Implement a single -page interface for managing BOM templates and versions.
   -Ensure users can easily clone BOM structures and specify necessary details like cost center and 
location.
2. **Cloning Process**:
   -Provide a button to clone BOM structures.
   -Allow users to select the plant and resource, which will automatically map to the correct cost center.
3. **Safety Checks**:
   -Implement a system to highlight any issues (e.g., yellow comments) and ensure BOMs are safe to use.Monday, August 25, 2025 1:07 PM
