2. Call SAP RFC or BAPI Functions (Best for GCP Integration)
Use when: You want to pull data into GCP or an external tool (ETL -style)
 BAPIs:
BAPI_BOM_GETHEADER•
BAPI_BOM_GETDETAIL•
RFC_READ_TABLE (generic table reader) •
 In GCP (via Cloud Function or Python Script):
python
CopyEdit
from pyrfc import Connection
conn = Connection(ashost='sap.yourcompany.com', sysnr='00', client='100',
                  user='youruser', passwd='yourpassword', lang='EN')
result = conn.call('RFC_READ_TABLE', QUERY_TABLE='MAST', DELIMITER='|',
                   OPTIONS=[{'TEXT': "MATNR = '10283180928' AND WERKS = 
'1176'"}])
  Output can be pushed to BigQuery or stored in Cloud Storage.
Option 2: Custom ABAP Report (for Scheduled Exports)
Ask your SAP dev team to create a simple ABAP program like:
abap
CopyEdit
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM STPO
  INTO lv_total
  WHERE VALID_TO = '99991231'
  AND LOEKZ = ''.
WRITE: / 'Total Active BOM Components:', lv_total.
Option 4: Export via RFC_READ_TABLE (For Automation)
If you're using Python or a tool that supports RFC:
python
CopyEdit
result = conn.call('RFC_READ_TABLE', QUERY_TABLE='STPO', 
                   OPTIONS=[{'TEXT': "VALID_TO = '99991231'"}])
print(f"Total valid components: {len(result['DATA'])}")Go to SQ01 1.

         SQL Trace  Find Out SAP s Internal Queries)
 Best for: Understanding SAP’s internal SQL queries .
How to do it:
Go to ST05 1.
Activate SQL Trace2.
Run CS03 or your BOM -related transaction. 3.
Stop the trace and analyze queries. 4.
Copy SAP's exact SQL queries for use in DBACOCKPIT or a custom ABAP program . 5.
✅Pros:Helps find SAP’s real SQL logic.
❌Cons:Can’t directly run queries, just traces them.
               Running Direct SQL Queries
 Best for: Users with HANA or DBA access .
How to do it:
Go to DBACOCKPIT 1.
Open SQL Editor 2.
Run a query like: SELECT MATNR, WERKS, STLNR FROM MAST WHERE MATNR = 
'10283180928' AND WERKS = '1176';3.
Export results.4.
✅Pros: Pure SQL, best for bulk queries .
❌Cons: Requires DBA access .
                       Get BOM via API
 Best for: Real -time BOM extraction .
How to call BAPI in Python:
from pyrfc import Connection
conn = Connection(ashost='sap_host', sysnr='00', client='100',
                  user='your_user', passwd='your_password', lang='EN')
result = conn.call('BAPI_BOM_GETDETAIL', MATERIAL='10283180928',
                   PLANT='1176', BOM_USAGE='6', ALTERNATIVE='01')
for item in result['BOMITEMS']:
    print(f"Component: {item['COMPONENT']} | Qty: {item['COMP_QTY']}")

❌Cons: Requires RFC access .

✅SAP Tables to Ingest into GCP
 1. MAST (Material to BOM Link)
Purpose : Links materials to their respective BOMs. •
Key Fields : •
MATNR–Material Number •
WERKS–Plant •
STLAN–BOM Usage •
STLAL–Alternative BOM •
STLNR–Bill of Material Number (foreign key to STKO ) •
 2. STKO (BOM Header)
Purpose : Stores BOM header data for each STLNR . •
Key Fields : •
STLNR–BOM Number •
WERKS–Plant •
STLTY–BOM Category (e.g., Mfor material BOMs) •
VALID_TO –Validity End Date •
 3. STPO (BOM Items)
Purpose : Contains the components (materials) that make up the BOM. •
Key Fields : •
STLNR–BOM Number (foreign key to STKO ) •
STLKN–BOM Item Counter •
IDNRK–Component Material Number •
 4. MARA (Material Master -Basic)
Purpose : Material -level metadata (used for validation). •
Key Fields : •
MATNR–Material Number •
MTART–Material Type •
MATKL–Material Group •
   (Plant Master Data)
Purpose : Details about plant locations. •
Key Fields : •
WERKS–Plant Code •
 Optional (if your BOM Portal uses these):
RECIPE (custom table) –if used in MySQL for recipe/BOM end dates •
Custom Z -tables–for additional flags, statuses, or relationships •
Change logs –if you want to track BOM changes over time •Tuesday, March 25, 2025 1:59 PM

 Strategy for Real -Time or Active BOM Comparison via GCP & SAP
 1. From SAP: Build a Real -Time View via RFC or CDS
Using:
MAST→ to get active BOM numbers•
STKO→ filter by DATUV <= today , LOEKZ = '' •
STPO→ pull current components ( VALID_TO = 99991231 ) •
Expose via:
RFC (BAPI or custom FM) , or •
CDS View or OData Service from S/4HANA •
Filter to just current BOMs by:
sql
CopyEdit
STKO.DATUV <= CURRENT_DATE AND STPO.VALID_TO = '99991231'
 2. From BOM Portal: Generate Matching Dataset
Use this MySQL query:
sql
CopyEdit
SELECT 
    m.MATERIAL_NUM AS MATNR,
    p.PLANT_CODE AS WERKS,
    r.VALID_START_DATE AS DATUV,
    sc.STEP_COMPONENT_ID AS IDNRK,
    sc.YIELD_QTY AS MENGE
FROM 
    RECIPE r
JOIN 
    PLANT_MATERIAL_XREF pmx ON r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
JOIN 
    MATERIAL m ON pmx.MATERIAL_ID = m.MATERIAL_ID
JOIN 
    PLANT p ON pmx.PLANT_ID = p.PLANT_ID
JOIN 
    STEP_COMPONENT sc ON sc.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
WHERE 
    r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL;
 3. Match SAP ↔BOM Portal in GCP or Python
Either in:
BigQuery (preferred for scale) •
Python (good for scriptable comparison) •
Dataform / dbt if you’re using a modeling layer in GCP •
Compare:
(MATNR, WERKS, DATUV, IDNRK, MENGE) between systems •
This gives you row-level mismatches or gaps in BOMs.
 Why This Works
Even if you don’t store STLNR , SAP maintains 1:1 BOM mapping via:
MAST.MATNR + WERKS•
Each MATNR + WERKS has a default BOM ( STLAL = 1 ) •
STKO/STPO maintain validity dates ( DATUV , VALID_TO ) which you can mirror with your 
VALID_START_DATE•
So even without SAP_BOM_NUM , you’re essentially re -deriving the current BOM snapshot dynamically.
✅Final Answer to Andre’s Question:
“Is there any other way besides storing SAP_BOM_NUM to reconcile?”
Yes —by leveraging:
Material Number•
Plant•
Valid From Date (or the latest active version) •
Component ID•
Quantity•
You can dynamically rebuild 1:1 relationships for active BOMs, especially if your MySQL or GCP dataset 
includes effective dates like VALID_START_DATE .
 Optional: If You Want to Backfill SAP_BOM_NUM
You could create a daily sync job to:
Pull active BOMs from SAP ( MAST , STKO ) 1.
Match by material + plant + valid date2.
Write matched SAP_BOM_NUM back to your RECIPE table for traceability 3.✅Option 3: SAP Query via SQ01 (Advanced Reporting)
If you want this to be reusable and schedulable, convert your SQVI query into SQ01 . You can then:
Schedule it with SM36 / SM37 •
Output to spool or download•
Email the results via background job•

Perfect —thanks for clarifying! If your team wants to reconcile BOM data using a composite key made 
up of :
Material + Plant + Recipe + Valid End Date
… then here’s how you should proceed. This method allows you to uniquely identify a BOM version
across BOM Portal and SAP —even without relying on SAP_BOM_NUM .
✅Updated Reconciliation Strategy (Composite Key: Material + Plant + Recipe + 
Valid End Date)
 Why This Key Works
This combination:
Material = BOM parent •
Plant= Where it’s used •
Recipe = BOM structure or variant (e.g., version of steps) •
Valid End Date = Version window •
Together, these act as a natural key for BOMs in both systems.
 Step -by-Step Plan for 1:1 Reconciliation
 1. From BOM Portal: Extract Composite Key
SELECT 
    m.MATERIAL_NUM,
    p.PLANT_CODE,
    r.RECIPE_ID,
    COALESCE(r.VALID_END_DATE, '9999 -12-31') AS VALID_END_DATE
FROM 
    RECIPE r
JOIN PLANT_MATERIAL_XREF pmx ON r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
JOIN MATERIAL m ON pmx.MATERIAL_ID = m.MATERIAL_ID
JOIN PLANT p ON pmx.PLANT_ID = p.PLANT_ID
WHERE 
    r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL OR r.VALID_END_DATE >= CURRENT_DATE;
 Output = your active BOMs in the Portal using the agreed composite key.
 2. From SAP: Extract Matching Composite Fields
If RECIPE_ID isn’t in SAP, use SAP’s own internal version fields like:
MATNR (Material) •
WERKS (Plant) •
STLNR/STKO.STLAL (Alternative BOM or version number) •
STKO.AEDAT or VALID_TO (to simulate VALID_END_DATE ) •
Use this logic in SE16N or SQVI :Friday, March 21, 2025 3:25 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 720    
Use this logic in SE16N or SQVI :
SELECT 
    MAST.MATNR, 
    MAST.WERKS, 
    STKO.STLNR,
    STKO.STLAL,
    STKO.VALID_TO
FROM 
    MAST
JOIN 
    STKO ON MAST.STLNR = STKO.STLNR
WHERE 
    STKO.VALID_TO = '99991231'  --active
 3. Compare Composite Keys
In Excel or Python: 
Combine keys from both sets: 
Portal : MATERIAL_NUM + PLANT_CODE + RECIPE_ID + VALID_END_DATE •
SAP: MATNR + WERKS + STLAL + VALID_TO••
Match them using a join or lookup•
Flag missing/mismatched entries••
 Bonus: Reconcile Step Components per Key
To drill deeper, join in the component level:
BOM Portal : STEP_COMPONENT by RECIPE_ID •
SAP: STPO by STLNR •
So now you’re comparing:
MATERIAL + PLANT + RECIPE + VALID_END_DATE + COMPONENT
That gives full 1:1 BOM + component -level reconciliation , even without BOM_NUM.

   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 722    
---
**Strategic Decision: Population of SAP_BOM_NUM**
---
**Introduction**
The population of SAP_BOM_NUM is a strategic decision that unlocks long -term flexibility, accuracy, and 
integration. Below is a clear and professional breakdown to discuss with your manager or SAP/business 
stakeholders.
---
**Why We Should Populate SAP_BOM_NUM**
**What is SAP_BOM_NUM?**
SAP_BOM_NUM is a reference field in the BOM Portal that stores SAP’s BOM number (STLNR), uniquely 
identifying a BOM in SAP.
---
**Top 5 Reasons to Populate SAP_BOM_NUM**
1. **Enables Direct 1:1 Link Between BOM Portal and SAP**
   -Currently, we use composite keys (Material + Plant + Recipe + Dates).
   -Populating SAP_BOM_NUM allows a direct join to SAP’s STPO, STKO, MAST tables.
   -This eliminates ambiguity and ensures precision.
2. **Enables Component -Level Reconciliation**
   -With SAP_BOM_NUM, you can:
     -Pull all components from SAP via STPO.
     -Compare directly to Portal’s STEP_COMPONENT.
   -No need to guess or simulate versions —you’re looking at the exact BOM structure from SAP.Friday, March 21, 2025 1:29 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 723    
3. **Improves Reporting & Auditing**
   -Allows stakeholders to trace a BOM in both systems using a shared ID.
   -Critical for costing, manufacturing, regulatory, and version control reviews.
   -Makes reconciliation and audit trail building 100x easier.
4. **Unlocks Integration with SAP APIs**
   -APIs like CSAP_MAT_BOM_READ, CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2 require STLNR (BOM num).
   -Without it, automation and synchronization pipelines hit a wall.
5. **Reduces Risk of Mismatched Data**
   -Prevents situations where Material + Plant + Recipe align, but component structure differs due to SAP 
versioning.
   -SAP_BOM_NUM anchors your data to the real source of truth.
---
**What It Will Take to Populate SAP_BOM_NUM**
| Task                  | Description                                             | Owner                |
|----------------------- |--------------------------------------------------------- |---------------------- |
| Identify Matching Logic | Use MATERIAL_NUM + PLANT_CODE to find matching STLNR from MAST | 
You + SAP Functional |
| Backfill Script       | Update RECIPE.SAP_BOM_NUM with matched STLNR            | DBA / Data 
Engineering |
| Historical Versions   | Optionally store in a history table if recipes map to multiple BOMs | Optional             
|
| Ongoing Sync          | Auto -populate SAP_BOM_NUM when new BOMs are created or updated | Dev / 
Integration    |
| Governance            | Add data validation rules to ensure only valid STLNR values are stored | App Team 
/ QA        |
---
**Example Mapping Query (One -Time Backfill)**
```sql
--Pseudocode: match based on material and plant
UPDATE RECIPE r
JOIN PLANT_MATERIAL_XREF pmx ON r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 724    
JOIN MAST sap ON sap.MATNR = pmx.MATERIAL_ID AND sap.WERKS = pmx.PLANT_ID
SET r.SAP_BOM_NUM = sap.STLNR
WHERE r.SAP_BOM_NUM IS NULL;
```
This will require aligning MATERIAL_NUM formatting (e.g., leading zeros) and ensuring data integrity.
---
**Strategic Benefit Summary**
| Benefit                | Impact                          |
|------------------------ |--------------------------------- |
| Accurate SAP –Portal joins | Enables full 1:1 reconciliation |
| Clean reporting        | Reduces manual reconciliation logic |
| Enables APIs           | Simplifies integration with SAP functions |
| Reduces human error    | No more guessing BOM structure by composite |
| Future -proof           | Makes audit, costing, and spec compliance seamless |
---
**Final Recommendation**
Populate SAP_BOM_NUM now, via a one -time backfill and implement logic to keep it updated going 
forward. This small data quality initiative will unlock massive value for reconciliation, automation, and 
analytics —and de -risk future system upgrades or integrations.

Description:
Currently, BOM_MYSQL is missing data in the following fields:
SAP_RECIPE_NUM•
SAP_BOM_NUM•
This task involves reconciling BOM_MYSQL with SAP by:
Identifying missing data by comparing BOM_MYSQL with following SAP tables 
STKO -(BOM Header) Table in SAP a.
STPO -(BOM item) Table in SAP b.
MARA -(General Material Data) Table in SAP c.
MAST -(Material to BOM Link) Table d.1.
Updating BOM_MYSQL to ensure that all SAP BOM details are reflected. 2.
Verifying a 1:1 mapping exists between BOM_MYSQL and SAP. 3.
Automating reconciliation queries where feasible. 4.
Acceptance Criteria:
BOM_MYSQL has all SAP BOM numbers ( SAP_BOM_NUM ) linked correctly. •
SAP_RECIPE_NUM and SAP_VERSION_NUM fields are populated for all relevant BOMs. •
No duplicate entries exist for SAP_BOM_NUM in BOM_MYSQL . •
SQL validation queries show full reconciliation.•
Query for Missing Data Identification:
sql
CopyEdit
SELECT 
    stko.STLNR AS Missing_BOM,
    stko.STLAL AS Missing_Recipe,
    stas.BOM_VERSN AS Missing_Version
FROM STKO stko
LEFT JOIN STAS stas ON stko.STLNR = stas.STLNR
LEFT JOIN BOM_MYSQL bm ON stko.STLNR = bm.SAP_BOM_NUM
WHERE bm.SAP_BOM_NUM IS NULL;
Query for Data Insertion:
sql
CopyEdit
INSERT INTO BOM_MYSQL (SAP_BOM_NUM, SAP_RECIPE_NUM, SAP_VERSION_NUM)
SELECT 
    stko.STLNR, 
    stko.STLAL, 
    stas.BOM_VERSN
FROM STKO stko
LEFT JOIN STAS stas ON stko.STLNR = stas.STLNR
LEFT JOIN BOM_MYSQL bm ON stko.STLNR = bm.SAP_BOM_NUM
WHERE bm.SAP_BOM_NUM IS NULL;
Query for Data Integrity Check:
sql
CopyEdit
SELECT SAP_BOM_NUM, COUNT(*) 
FROM BOM_MYSQL 
GROUP BY SAP_BOM_NUM 
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

✅How We’ll Accomplish SAP & BOM_MySQL Ingestion and 1:1 Reconciliation 
in GCP
 1. What Needs to Be Ingested
 From SAP →GCP
These are the required tables for BOM structure:
SAP Table Purpose Key Fields
MAST Links material to BOM MATNR , WERKS , STLAN , STLAL , STLNR
STKO BOM header details STLNR , WERKS , STLTY , VALID_TO
STPO BOM components STLNR , STLKN , IDNRK , MENGE
MARA Material master data MATNR , MTART , etc.
T001W Plant master WERKS , NAME1 , etc.
STAS (Optional) BOM version info STLNR , BOM_VERSN
✅These should be ingested into BigQuery (e.g. hub.sap_mast , hub.sap_stko , etc.).
 From BOM_MySQL →GCP
These are needed for BOM Portal context:
BOM_MySQL Table Purpose
RECIPE Recipe metadata & validity
MATERIAL Material master for BOMP
PLANT Plant metadata
PLANT_MATERIAL_XREF Join between plant and material
RECIPE_AUDIT / COMPONENT_EDIT_AUDIT Optional: trace changes
FORMULATION / FORMULATION_COMPONENT Optional: for recipe structure detail
✅Ingest these into BigQuery as:
bom.recipe•
bom.material•
bom.plant•
bom.plant_material_xref•
 2. How We’ll Establish a 1:1 Relationship
 Define Composite Keys
SAP (STKO + MAST) BOM_MySQL (RECIPE)Tuesday, March 25, 2025 2:17 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 731    
MATNR MATERIAL_NUM
WERKS PLANT_CODE
STLAL SAP_VERSION_NUM (or similar)
STLNR SAP_BOM_NUM
VALID_TO VALID_END_DATE
You’ll test:
(MATNR + WERKS + STLAL + VALID_TO) = (MATERIAL_NUM + PLANT_CODE + 
SAP_VERSION_NUM + VALID_END_DATE)
✅If this holds true across data sets, you have a reliable 1:1 mapping for reconciliation.
 3. How to Query the Data in BigQuery for Reconciliation
Once ingested, run a reconciliation query like:
SELECT
  sap.MATNR AS sap_material,
  sap.WERKS AS sap_plant,
  sap.STLAL AS sap_version,
  stko.VALID_TO AS sap_valid_to,
  stko.STLNR AS sap_bom_num,
  recipe.MATERIAL_NUM AS bom_material,
  recipe.PLANT_CODE AS bom_plant,
  recipe.SAP_VERSION_NUM AS bom_version,
  recipe.VALID_END_DATE AS bom_valid_to,
  recipe.SAP_BOM_NUM AS bom_bom_num,
  CASE
    WHEN stko.STLNR = recipe.SAP_BOM_NUM THEN 'Match'
    ELSE 'Mismatch'
  END AS bom_match_status
FROM
  `hub.sap_mast` sap
JOIN
  `hub.sap_stko` stko
  ON sap.STLNR = stko.STLNR AND sap.WERKS = stko.WERKS
LEFT JOIN
  `bom.recipe` recipe
  ON sap.MATNR = recipe.MATERIAL_NUM
  AND sap.WERKS = recipe.PLANT_CODE
  AND sap.STLAL = recipe.SAP_VERSION_NUM
  AND stko.VALID_TO = recipe.VALID_END_DATE

the BOM (Bill of Materials) you are reconciling. Here are key SAP tables that may be relevant:
1. Master BOM Data
STKO (Bill of Material Header Table) –Contains high -level BOM details like validity dates and 
usage.•
STPO (Bill of Material Items Table) –Stores BOM components and their relationships. •
MAST (Material to BOM Link Table) –Links BOMs to material masters. •
Recommended Approach

ZTFIT_PARM or similar.3.

BOM Version BOM_VERSNSELECT 
    --Material Master Data
    MARA.MATNR AS Material,
    MARA.ERSDA AS Created_On,
    MARA.DATAB AS Valid_From,
    MARA.LIQDT AS Valid_To,
    --BOM Header Data
    STKO.STLNR AS BOM_Number,
    STKO.STLAL AS Alternative_BOM,
    STKO.DATUV AS BOM_Valid_From,
    STKO.ANDAT AS BOM_Created_On,
    STKO.AEDAT AS BOM_Changed_On,
    STKO.STLST AS BOM_Status,
    STKO.WRKAN AS BOM_Created_In_Plant,
    STKO.VALID_TO AS BOM_Valid_To,
    --BOM Item Data
    STPO.DATUV AS BOM_Item_Valid_From,
    STPO.ANDAT AS BOM_Item_Created_On,
    STPO.AEDAT AS BOM_Item_Changed_On,
    STPO.VALID_TO AS BOM_Item_Valid_To,
    --Material to BOM Link
    MAST.STLAN AS BOM_Usage,
    MAST.STLNR AS MAST_BOM_Number,
    MAST.STLAL AS MAST_Alt_BOM,
    MAST.ANDAT AS MAST_Created_On,
    MAST.AEDAT AS MAST_Changed_On,
    --BOM Status Data
    STAS.STLNR AS STAS_BOM_Number,
    STAS.STLAL AS STAS_Alt_BOM,
    STAS.DATUV AS STAS_Valid_From,
    STAS.ANDAT AS STAS_Created_On,
    STAS.AEDAT AS STAS_Changed_On,
    STAS.BOM_VERSN AS BOM_Version
FROM MARA
LEFT JOIN MARC ON MARA.MATNR = MARC.MATNR
LEFT JOIN STKO ON MARA.MATNR = STKO.STLNR
LEFT JOIN STPO ON STKO.STLNR = STPO.STLNR
LEFT JOIN MAST ON MARA.MATNR = MAST.MATNR
LEFT JOIN STAS ON STKO.STLNR = STAS.STLNRSAP_VERSION_NUM
SAP_RECIPE_NUM
SAP_BOM_NUM
These tables are missing data in BOM_MYSQL
To establish a 1:1 relationship between BOM_MYSQL and SAP data, follow these steps:
1. Identify the Matching Keys
To reconcile BOM_MYSQL with SAP tables, determine the unique identifiers that exist in both systems.
SAP Tables and Keys : 
STKO.STLNR (BOM Number) = SAP_BOM_NUM •
STKO.STLAL (Alternative BOM) = SAP_RECIPE_NUM •
MARA.MATNR (Material Number) = Material in BOM_MYSQL •
STAS.BOM_VERSN (BOM Version) = SAP_VERSION_NUM ••
If BOM_MYSQL lacks these values , they need to be mapped or populated using SAP data.
2. Build a Query to Reconcile BOM_MYSQL with SAP
We need to compare SAP_BOM_NUM, SAP_VERSION_NUM, SAP_RECIPE_NUM in BOM_MYSQL with 
the corresponding SAP fields .
SELECT 
    bm.SAP_BOM_NUM AS BOM_MYSQL_BOM,
    bm.SAP_RECIPE_NUM AS BOM_MYSQL_Recipe,
    bm.SAP_VERSION_NUM AS BOM_MYSQL_Version,
    
    --SAP Data
    stko.STLNR AS SAP_BOM_NUM,
    stko.STLAL AS SAP_RECIPE_NUM,
    stas.BOM_VERSN AS SAP_VERSION_NUM
    
FROM BOM_MYSQL bm
LEFT JOIN STKO stko ON bm.SAP_BOM_NUM = stko.STLNR
LEFT JOIN STAS stas ON stko.STLNR = stas.STLNR AND bm.SAP_VERSION_NUM = 
stas.BOM_VERSN
3. Identify Missing Data
If SAP_BOM_NUM , SAP_VERSION_NUM , or SAP_RECIPE_NUM are NULL in BOM_MYSQL but exist 
in SAP, they need to be inserted into BOM_MYSQL.•
Use this query to identify missing records : •
SELECT 
    stko.STLNR AS Missing_BOM,
    stko.STLAL AS Missing_Recipe,
    stas.BOM_VERSN AS Missing_Version
FROM STKO stko
LEFT JOIN STAS stas ON stko.STLNR = stas.STLNR
LEFT JOIN BOM_MYSQL bm ON stko.STLNR = bm.SAP_BOM_NUM
WHERE bm.SAP_BOM_NUM IS NULL;
4. Populate Missing Data in BOM_MYSQL
If there are missing entries in BOM_MYSQL, populate them:
INSERT INTO BOM_MYSQL (SAP_BOM_NUM, SAP_RECIPE_NUM, SAP_VERSION_NUM)
SELECT 
    stko.STLNR, 
    stko.STLAL, 
    stas.BOM_VERSN
FROM STKO stko
LEFT JOIN STAS stas ON stko.STLNR = stas.STLNR
LEFT JOIN BOM_MYSQL bm ON stko.STLNR = bm.SAP_BOM_NUM
WHERE bm.SAP_BOM_NUM IS NULL;
5. Verify 1:1 Relationship
Ensure no duplicates exist in BOM_MYSQL for each SAP_BOM_NUM . •
Run:•
SELECT SAP_BOM_NUM, COUNT(*) 
FROM BOM_MYSQL 
GROUP BY SAP_BOM_NUM SAP to BOM P Tables
Wednesday, March 19, 2025 11:21 AM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 735    
GROUP BY SAP_BOM_NUM 
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
If duplicates exist, review and clean up the data.
Final Outcome
This process will synchronize BOM_MYSQL with SAP . •
Missing data will be identified and inserted . •
A 1:1 relationship between BOM_MYSQL and SAP will be established. •

provided from your queries and requirements.
### Google BigQuery Configuration
1. **Billing Project ID (optional)**:
   -`prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee`
2. **Use Storage API (optional)**:
   -`true`
3. **Connection Timeout Duration**:
   -`0.00:05:00` (5 minutes)
4. **Command Timeout Duration**:
   -`0.00:10:00` (10 minutes)
5. **Project ID (optional)**:
   -`prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee`
6. **SQL Statement (optional, requires Project ID)**:
   ```sql
   --Step 1: Extract Valid BOMs from BOM Portal
   WITH bom_data AS (
       SELECT
           p.PLANT_CODE,
           m.MATERIAL_NUM
       FROM
           `prod -2134 -
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.recipe_current` r
       INNER JOIN
           `prod -2134 -
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_material_xref_current` pmx
       ON
           pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
       INNER JOIN
           `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.material_current` m
       ON
           m.MATERIAL_ID = pmx.MATERIAL_ID
       INNER JOIN
           `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_current` p
       ON
           p.PLANT_ID = pmx.PLANT_ID
       WHERE
           r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL
   ),
   --Step 2: Extract Valid BOMs from SAP
   sap_data AS (
       SELECT
           mast.WERKS AS PLANT_CODE,
           REGEXP_REPLACE(mast.MATNR, r'^0+', '') AS MATERIAL_NUM
       FROM
           `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_internal.mast_current` mast
       INNER JOIN
           `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_confidential.stko_current` stko
       ON
           mast.STLNR = stko.STLNRPowerBI
Tuesday, April 08, 2025 1:13 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 742    
       INNER JOIN
           `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_confidential.stpo_current` stpo
       ON
           mast.STLNR = stpo.STLNR
       WHERE
           stko.VALID_TO = '9999 -12-31' AND stko.STLTY = 'M'
   ),
   --Step 3: Identify Discrepancies
   discrepancies AS (
       SELECT DISTINCT
           COALESCE(bom.PLANT_CODE, sap.PLANT_CODE) AS PLANT_CODE,
           bom.MATERIAL_NUM AS bom_material_num,
           sap.MATERIAL_NUM AS sap_material_num,
           CASE
               WHEN bom.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL THEN 'Not on BOM'
               WHEN sap.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL THEN 'Not on SAP'
           END AS status
       FROM
           bom_data bom
       FULL OUTER JOIN
           sap_data sap
       ON
           bom.PLANT_CODE = sap.PLANT_CODE AND bom.MATERIAL_NUM = sap.MATERIAL_NUM
       WHERE
           bom.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL OR sap.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL
   )
   --Step 4: Combine Results and Add Summary Row
   SELECT
       PLANT_CODE,
       bom_material_num,
       sap_material_num,
       status
   FROM
       discrepancies
   UNION ALL
   SELECT
       'TOTAL' AS PLANT_CODE,
       NULL AS bom_material_num,
       NULL AS sap_material_num,
       CONCAT('Not on BOM: ', COUNTIF(status = 'Not on BOM'), ', Not on SAP: ', COUNTIF(status = 'Not on 
SAP')) AS status
   FROM
       discrepancies
   ORDER BY
       PLANT_CODE, status;
   ```
### Explanation:
1. **Billing Project ID**:
   -Set to the project ID where billing is managed: `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee`.
2. **Use Storage API**:
   -Set to `true` to utilize the Storage API for improved performance.
3. **Connection Timeout Duration**:
   -Set to `5 minutes` to allow sufficient time for establishing a connection.
4. **Command Timeout Duration**:
   -Set to `10 minutes` to allow sufficient time for executing the command.
5. **Project ID**:
   -Set to the project ID where data is stored: `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee`.
6. **SQL Statement**:
   -The SQL statement provided extracts and reconciles BOM data between the BOM Portal and SAP, 
identifies discrepancies, and generates a summary row.
By filling out the configuration window with these details, you will be able to establish a connection to 
Google BigQuery, execute the necessary queries, and generate the required Power BI report for BOM 
data reconciliation

The following query is great for reconciliation on its own but is lacking date/time info from the Dice Ingestion 
timestamp
WITH bom_data AS (
    SELECT 
        p.PLANT_CODE ,
        m.MATERIAL_NUM
    FROM
        `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.recipe_current` r
    INNER JOIN
        `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_material_xref_current` pmx
    ON
        pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
    INNER JOIN
        `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.material_current` m
    ON
        m.MATERIAL_ID = pmx.MATERIAL_ID
    INNER JOIN
        `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_current` p
    ON
        p.PLANT_ID = pmx.PLANT_ID
    WHERE
        r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL
),
sap_data AS (
    SELECT
        mast.WERKS AS PLANT_CODE ,
        REGEXP_REPLACE (mast.MATNR , r'^0+' , '') AS MATERIAL_NUM
    FROM
        `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_internal.mast_current` mast
    INNER JOIN
        `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_confidential.stko_current` stko
    ON
        mast.STLNR = stko.STLNR
    INNER JOIN
        `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_confidential.stpo_current` stpo
    ON
        mast.STLNR = stpo.STLNR
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 757    
        mast.STLNR = stpo.STLNR
    WHERE
        stko.VALID_TO = '9999 -12-31'
        AND stko.STLTY = 'M'
),
discrepancies AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        COALESCE (bom.PLANT_CODE , sap.PLANT_CODE ) AS PLANT_CODE ,
        bom.MATERIAL_NUM AS bom_material_num ,
        sap.MATERIAL_NUM AS sap_material_num ,
        CASE
            WHEN bom.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL THEN 'Not on BOM'
            WHEN sap.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL THEN 'Not on SAP'
        END AS status
    FROM
        bom_data bom
    FULL OUTER JOIN
        sap_data sap
    ON
        bom.PLANT_CODE = sap.PLANT_CODE AND bom.MATERIAL_NUM = sap.MATERIAL_NUM
    WHERE
        bom.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL OR sap.MATERIAL_NUM IS NULL
)
SELECT 
    PLANT_CODE ,
    bom_material_num ,
    sap_material_num ,
    status
FROM 
    discrepancies
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    'TOTAL' AS PLANT_CODE ,
    NULL AS bom_material_num ,
    NULL AS sap_material_num ,
    CONCAT ('Not on BOM: ' , COUNTIF (status = 'Not on BOM' ), ', Not on SAP: ' , COUNTIF (status = 'Not on SAP' )) AS status
FROM 
    discrepancies
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Page 758    
ORDER BY 
    PLANT_CODE , status ;

SELECT DISTINCT
  r.RECIPE_ID                              AS RecipeID,
  p.PLANT_CODE                             AS PlantCode,
  r.BOM_TYPE                               AS BOMType,
  CASE
    WHEN r.BOM_TYPE = 'production' THEN r.SAP_VERSION_NUM + 31
    WHEN r.BOM_TYPE = 'finance'    THEN r.SAP_VERSION_NUM + 1
  END                                      AS Version,
  m.MATERIAL_NUM                            AS Primary_Material,
  r.THROUGHPUT_QTY                          AS Throughput,
  r.CREW_QTY                                AS Crewing,
  CONCAT_WS(' -', cfs.FORMULATION_STEP_CODE, cfs.FORMULATION_STEP_VERSION) AS StepCode,
  cfs.FORMULATION_STEP_DESCR               AS Step_Description,
  cm.MATERIAL_NUM                          AS Component_Material,
  cm.MATERIAL_DESCR                        AS Component_Description,
  sc.USAGE_PROBABILITY_PCT AS Usage_PCT,
  sc.COMP_SCRAP_PCT                         AS Scrap_Percentage,
  ict.ITEM_CODE_TYPE_DESCR                 AS Item_Category,
  u.UOM_DESCR                              AS UOM_Description,
  r.VALID_START_DATE,
  r.VALID_END_DATE 
FROM RECIPE r
  INNER JOIN PLANT_MATERIAL_XREF pmx ON r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
  INNER JOIN PLANT p ON pmx.PLANT_ID = p.PLANT_ID
  INNER JOIN MATERIAL m ON pmx.MATERIAL_ID = m.MATERIAL_ID
  INNER JOIN RECIPE_FORMULATION_XREF rfx ON r.RECIPE_ID = rfx.RECIPE_ID
  INNER JOIN FORMULATION_STEP fs ON rfx.FORMULATION_STEP_ID = fs.FORMULATION_STEP_ID
  INNER JOIN CORP_FORMULATION_STEP cfs ON fs.CORP_FORMULATION_STEP_ID = 
cfs.CORP_FORMULATION_STEP_ID
  INNER JOIN STEP_COMPONENT_GROUP scg ON scg.FORMULATION_STEP_ID = 
fs.FORMULATION_STEP_ID
  INNER JOIN STEP_COMPONENT sc ON sc.STEP_COMPONENT_GROUP_ID = 
scg.STEP_COMPONENT_GROUP_ID
  INNER JOIN PLANT_MATERIAL_XREF cpmx ON sc.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = cpmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
  INNER JOIN MATERIAL cm ON cpmx.MATERIAL_ID = cm.MATERIAL_ID
  INNER JOIN ITEM_CODE_TYPE ict ON ict.ITEM_CODE_TYPE_ID = sc.ITEM_CODE_TYPE_ID
  INNER JOIN MATERIAL_UOM_XREF mux ON mux.MATERIAL_UOM_ID = sc.MATERIAL_UOM_ID
  INNER JOIN UOM u ON u.UOM_ID = mux.UOM_ID
  WHERE r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL
  AND m.MATERIAL_NUM  = 10000053149
    and rfx.INVN_PT_IND = 1
    and r.BOM_TYPE = 'finance'
    and p.plant_code = 1506
  AND sc.USAGE_PROBABILITY_PCT <> 0SAP Query
WITH latest_stko AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT
      stko.stlnr ,
      stko.stlty ,
      stko.datuv AS bom_valid_from_date ,
      stko.valid_to AS bom_valid_to_date ,
      stko.loekz AS bom_deletion_flag ,
      mast.matnr ,
      mast.werks ,
      mast.stlan ,
      mast.stlal ,
      ROW_NUMBER () OVER (
        PARTITION BY mast.matnr , mast.werks , mast.stlan , mast.stlal
        ORDER BY stko.datuv DESC
      ) AS rn
    FROM `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_confidential.stko_current` stko
    JOIN `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_internal.mast_current` mast
      ON stko.stlnr = mast.stlnr
    WHERE stko.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'
    and stko.stlty = 'M'
    and stko.lkenz is null
  )
  WHERE rn= 1
),
latest_stpo AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT
      stpo.meins ,
      stpo.stlnr ,
      stpo.posnr ,
      stpo.idnrk ,
      stpo.menge ,
      stpo.ewahr ,
      stpo.ausch ,
      stpo.datuv AS component_valid_from_date ,
      stpo.valid_to AS component_valid_to_date ,
      stpo.lkenz AS component_deletion_flag ,
      stpo.postp AS item_category ,
      ROW_NUMBER () OVER (
        PARTITION BY stpo.stlnr , stpo.posnr , stpo.idnrk
        ORDER BY stpo.datuv DESC
      ) AS rn
    FROM `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_confidential.stpo_current` stpo
    WHERE stpo.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'
    AND stpo.lkenz is null
    AND stpo.stlty = 'M'
  )
  WHERE rn= 1
),
pi05_step AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT
      plpo.plnnr ,
      plpo.plnty ,
      plpo.werks ,
      plpo.bmsch ,
      plpo.vgw03 ,
      ROW_NUMBER () OVER (
        PARTITION BY plpo.plnnr , plpo.plnty , plpo.werks
        ORDER BY plpo.datuv DESC
      ) AS rn
    FROM `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_internal.plpo_current` plpo
    WHERE plpo.steus = 'PI05'
      AND plpo.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'
      AND plpo.loekz IS NULL
      AND plpo.plnty = '2'
  )
  WHERE rn= 1
)

--============================================================================
--Reconciliation of BOM -Portal vs. SAP, INCLUDING alternate -BOM versions
--with recipe_formulation_xref_current enforced
--============================================================================
WITH
--1) Portal data (only recipes with at least one formulation entry)
bom_data AS (
  SELECT
    r.RECIPE_ID ,
    p.PLANT_CODE ,
    m.MATERIAL_NUM ,
    m.MATERIAL_DESCR ,
    m.MATERIAL_TYPE ,
    r.BOM_TYPE ,
    CASE
      WHEN r.BOM_TYPE = 'Production' THEN CAST (r.SAP_VERSION_NUM + 31 AS STRING )
      WHEN r.BOM_TYPE = 'Finance'     THEN LPAD (CAST (r.SAP_VERSION_NUM + 1 AS STRING ), 2, '0')
      ELSE r.BOM_TYPE
    END AS BOM_VERSION ,
    DATE (r.VALID_START_DATE ) AS VALID_START_DATE
  FROM
    `prod -2134 -
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.recipe_current`        AS r
  INNER JOIN
    `prod -2134 -
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_material_xref_current`        AS pmx
    ON r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
  INNER JOIN
    `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.material_current`                    
AS m
    ON pmx.MATERIAL_ID = m.MATERIAL_ID
  INNER JOIN
    `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_current`                       
AS p
    ON pmx.PLANT_ID = p.PLANT_ID
  INNER JOIN
    `mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.recipe_formulation_xref_current` AS rfx
    ON rfx.RECIPE_ID = r.RECIPE_ID
  WHERE
    r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL
    AND r.BOM_TYPE       IN ('Production' ,'Finance' )
    AND rfx.INVN_PT_IND = 1
    AND NOT (m.MATERIAL_TYPE LIKE 'ZCWP%' AND m.MATERIAL_DESCR LIKE 'PH -%')
),Component Level
Monday, July 07, 2025 9:40 AM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 762    
--2) SAP CS03 data
sap_data AS (
  SELECT
    mast.WERKS                              AS PLANT_CODE ,
    REGEXP_REPLACE (mast.MATNR , r'^0+' , '') AS MATERIAL_NUM ,
    makt.MAKTX                              AS SAP_MATERIAL_DESCR ,
    mara.MTART                              AS MATERIAL_TYPE ,
    CASE mast.STLAN
      WHEN '1' THEN 'Production'
      WHEN '6' THEN 'Finance'
    END                                     AS BOM_TYPE ,
    mast.STLAL                              AS BOM_VERSION ,
    DATE (stko.DATUV )                       AS VALID_START_DATE ,
    stko.STLNR                              AS SAP_BOM_NUM
  FROM
    `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_internal.mast_current`   AS mast
  JOIN
    `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_confidential.stko_current` AS stko
    ON mast.MANDT = stko.MANDT
   AND mast.STLNR = stko.STLNR
   AND mast.STLAL = stko.STLAL
  JOIN
    `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_internal.makt_current`   AS makt
    ON mast.MATNR = makt.MATNR
  JOIN
    `prod -2134 -entdatalake -5938ee.hana_p0a_tenv_s4_internal.mara_current`                               AS mara
    ON mast.MATNR = mara.MATNR
  WHERE
    stko.STLTY = 'M'
    AND stko.VALID_TO = '9999 -12-31'
    AND mast.STLAN IN ('1','6')
    AND NOT (mara.MTART LIKE 'ZCWP%' AND makt.MAKTX LIKE 'PH -%')
),
--3) All unique keys
all_keys AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT PLANT_CODE , MATERIAL_NUM , MATERIAL_TYPE , BOM_TYPE , BOM_VERSION
    FROM bom_data
  UNION DISTINCT
  SELECT DISTINCT PLANT_CODE , MATERIAL_NUM , MATERIAL_TYPE , BOM_TYPE , BOM_VERSION
    FROM sap_data
),
--4) Flag mismatches
discrepancies AS (
  SELECT
    ak.PLANT_CODE ,
    ak.MATERIAL_NUM ,
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 763    
    ak.MATERIAL_NUM ,
    ak.MATERIAL_TYPE ,
    ak.BOM_TYPE ,
    ak.BOM_VERSION ,
    bd.RECIPE_ID ,
    bd.MATERIAL_DESCR ,
    sd.SAP_MATERIAL_DESCR ,
    sd.SAP_BOM_NUM ,
    CASE
      WHEN bd.RECIPE_ID IS NULL THEN 'Not on BOM Portal'
      WHEN sd.SAP_BOM_NUM IS NULL THEN 'Not on SAP'
      ELSE 'Match'
    END AS discrepancy
  FROM all_keys ak
  LEFT JOIN bom_data bd
    ON bd.PLANT_CODE     = ak.PLANT_CODE
   AND bd.MATERIAL_NUM   = ak.MATERIAL_NUM
   AND bd.MATERIAL_TYPE = ak.MATERIAL_TYPE
   AND bd.BOM_TYPE       = ak.BOM_TYPE
   AND bd.BOM_VERSION    = ak.BOM_VERSION
  LEFT JOIN sap_data sd
    ON sd.PLANT_CODE     = ak.PLANT_CODE
   AND sd.MATERIAL_NUM   = ak.MATERIAL_NUM
   AND sd.MATERIAL_TYPE = ak.MATERIAL_TYPE
   AND sd.BOM_TYPE       = ak.BOM_TYPE
   AND sd.BOM_VERSION    = ak.BOM_VERSION
)
--5) Return only the discrepancies, with both material descriptions
SELECT
  ROW_NUMBER () OVER (ORDER BY PLANT_CODE , MATERIAL_NUM , BOM_TYPE , BOM_VERSION ) AS 
row_id ,
  ANY_VALUE (RECIPE_ID )           AS recipe_id ,
  PLANT_CODE ,
  MATERIAL_NUM ,
  COALESCE (ANY_VALUE (MATERIAL_DESCR ), ANY_VALUE (SAP_MATERIAL_DESCR )) AS material_descr ,
  MATERIAL_TYPE ,
  BOM_TYPE ,
  BOM_VERSION ,
  ANY_VALUE (SAP_BOM_NUM )         AS sap_bom_number ,
  discrepancy
FROM discrepancies
WHERE discrepancy <> 'Match'
GROUP BY PLANT_CODE , MATERIAL_NUM , MATERIAL_TYPE , BOM_TYPE , BOM_VERSION , discrepancy
ORDER BY PLANT_CODE , MATERIAL_NUM , BOM_TYPE , BOM_VERSION ;

--Define a Common Table Expression (CTE) to extract BOM data from internal MySQL sources
WITH bom_data AS (
  SELECT
    r.RECIPE_ID AS RecipeID ,  --Unique identifier for the recipe
    p.PLANT_CODE AS PlantCode ,  --Plant code from the plant table
    r.BOM_TYPE AS BOMType ,  --BOM type (e.g., production or finance)
    --Calculate SAP version based on BOM type and pad it to 2 digits
    LPAD (
      CAST (
        CASE
          WHEN LOWER (r.BOM_TYPE )= 'production' THEN COALESCE (r.SAP_VERSION_NUM , 0) + 31
          WHEN LOWER (r.BOM_TYPE )= 'finance' THEN COALESCE (r.SAP_VERSION_NUM , 0) + 1
          ELSE NULL
        END AS STRING
      ), 2, '0'
    ) AS SAPVersion ,
    --Pad material number to 18 digits
    LPAD (m.MATERIAL_NUM , 18, '0') AS Primary_Material_Padded ,
    m.MATERIAL_TYPE ,  --Material type
    m.MATERIAL_DESCR ,  --Material description
    r.THROUGHPUT_QTY AS Throughput ,  --Throughput quantity from BOM
    r.CREW_QTY AS Crewing   --Crewing quantity from BOM
  FROM `mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.recipe_current` r
  --Join to get plant -material relationship
  INNER JOIN `mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_material_xref_current` pmx ON 
r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
  --Join to get plant details
  INNER JOIN `mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_current` p ON pmx.PLANT_ID = 
p.PLANT_ID
  --Join to get material details
  INNER JOIN `mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.material_current` m ON pmx.MATERIAL_ID = 
m.MATERIAL_ID
  --Join to get formulation indicator
  INNER JOIN `mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.recipe_formulation_xref_current` rfx ON 
r.RECIPE_ID = rfx.RECIPE_ID
  WHERE r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL   --Only include active recipes
    AND rfx.INVN_PT_IND = 1  --Only include inventory point indicators
    AND NOT (m.MATERIAL_TYPE LIKE 'ZCWP%' AND m.MATERIAL_DESCR LIKE 'PH -%')  --Exclude certain 
material types
),
--Define a CTE to extract SAP data
sap_data AS (
  --Nested CTE to get the latest valid PI05 step data
  WITH pi05_step AS (
    SELECT
      plpo.plnnr ,  --Routing group
      plpo.plnty ,  --Routing type
      plpo.werks ,  --Plant
      plpo.bmsch AS PI05_Throughput ,  --Throughput from SAP
      plpo.vgw03 AS PI05_Crewing   --Crewing from SAP
    FROM `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_internal.plpo_current` plpo
    WHERE plpo.steus = 'PI05'   --Only PI05 control key
      AND plpo.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'  --Valid records
      AND plpo.loekz IS NULL   --Not deleted
      AND plpo.plnty = '2'  --Only routing type 2
      AND plpo.datuv = (  --Get the most recent valid -from date
        SELECT MAX (plpo2.datuv )
        FROM `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_internal.plpo_current` plpo2
        WHERE plpo2.plnnr = plpo.plnnr
          AND plpo2.plnty = plpo.plnty
          AND plpo2.werks = plpo.werks
          AND plpo2.steus = 'PI05'
          AND plpo2.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'
          AND plpo2.loekz IS NULL
      )
  )
  --Main SAP data extraction
  SELECT 
    mapl.werks AS Plant ,  --Plant
    LPAD (mapl.matnr , 18, '0') AS Material_Number_Padded ,  --Padded material number
    mapl.plnnr AS Recipe_Group ,  --Routing group
    mapl.plnal AS Recipe_Group_Counter ,  --Routing group counter
    plmz.stlnr AS BOM_Number ,  --BOM number
    LPAD (plmz.stlal , 2, '0') AS BOM_Alt ,  --BOM alternative padded
    mkal.verid as Prod_Version ,  --Production version
    pi05_step.PI05_Throughput AS Throughput ,  --Throughput from SAP
    pi05_step.PI05_Crewing AS Crewing   --Crewing from SAP
  FROM `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_internal.mapl_current` mapl
  --Join to BOM header
  INNER JOIN `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_internal.plmz_current` plmz 
    ON mapl.plnty = plmz.plnty AND mapl.plnnr = plmz.plnnr AND mapl.plnal = plmz.plnal 
  --Join to PI05 step data
  INNER JOIN pi05_step 
    ON mapl.plnnr = pi05_step.plnnr AND mapl.plnty = pi05_step.plnty AND mapl.werks = pi05
_step.werks
  --Join to production version
  INNER JOIN `bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_05104f_s4hana_confidential.mkal_current` mkal 
    ON mkal.matnr = mapl.matnr AND mkal.werks = mapl.WERKS AND mkal.bdatu = '9999 -12-31'
  WHERE mapl.plnty = '2'  --Routing type 2
    AND mapl.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'  --Valid records
    AND plmz.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'
    AND plmz.stlty = 'M'  --BOM type M
    AND mapl.LOEKZ IS NULL   --Not deleted
    AND plmz.loekz IS NULL
),
--Reconciliation CTE to compare BOM and SAP data
reconciliation AS (
  SELECT
    b.RecipeID ,
    s.Recipe_Group ,
    s.Recipe_Group_Counter ,
    s.BOM_Number ,
    b.BOMType ,
    b.SAPVersion AS BOM_Version ,
    b.MATERIAL_TYPE ,
    b.Primary_Material_Padded AS Material_Number ,
    b.MATERIAL_DESCR AS Description ,
    b.Throughput AS Throughput_BOM ,
    s.Throughput AS Throughput_SAP ,
    --Check if throughput values match within a tolerance
    CASE
      WHEN ABS (b.Throughput -s.Throughput ) > 0.001 THEN 'Not Matched' ELSE 'Matched'
    END AS Throughput_Check ,
    b.Crewing AS Crewing_BOM ,
    s.Crewing AS Crewing_SAP ,
    --Check if crewing values match within a tolerance
    CASE
      WHEN ABS (b.Crewing -s.Crewing ) > 0.001 THEN 'Not Matched' ELSE 'Matched'
    END AS Crewing_Check ,
    --Determine the type of discrepancy
    CASE
      WHEN s.Material_Number_Padded IS NULL THEN 'Missing in SAP'
      WHEN b.Primary_Material_Padded IS NULL THEN 'Missing in BOM' 
      WHEN b.Throughput IS NOT NULL AND s.Throughput IS NOT NULL AND ABS (b.Throughput -
s.Throughput ) > 0.001 
           AND b.Crewing IS NOT NULL AND s.Crewing IS NOT NULL AND ABS (b.Crewing -s.Crewing ) > 0.001 
THEN 'Throughput, Crewing'
      WHEN b.Throughput IS NOT NULL AND s.Throughput IS NOT NULL AND ABS (b.Throughput -
s.Throughput ) > 0.001 THEN 'Throughput'
      WHEN b.Crewing IS NOT NULL AND s.Crewing IS NOT NULL AND ABS (b.Crewing -s.Crewing ) > 0.001 
THEN 'Crewing'
      ELSE 'No Discrepancies Exist'
    END AS Discrepancies
  FROM bom_data b
  --Left join to retain all BOM records and match with SAP
  LEFT JOIN sap_data s 
    ON b.PlantCode = s.Plant
    AND b.SAPVersion = s.BOM_Alt
    AND b.SAPVersion = RIGHT (s.Prod_Version , 2)
    AND b.Primary_Material_Padded = s.Material_Number_Padded
)
--Final selection: filter and deduplicate discrepancies
SELECT *
FROM (
  SELECT *,
         --Assign row numbers to group by material, BOM type, and versionWhat the Query Accomplishes
Copilot
This SQL query performs a detailed reconciliation between two systems —a BOM (Bill of Materials) 
system and SAP —to identify discrepancies in throughput and crewing values for materials used in 
manufacturing processes. The goal is to ensure that both systems are aligned in terms of how they 
define and quantify production processes.
 Step -by-Step Breakdown
1. bom_data CTE: Extract BOM System Data
This section pulls data from a MySQL -based BOM system. It focuses on active recipes and materials that 
are relevant for inventory and production.
Recipe and Plant Info: Extracts the recipe ID and plant code.
BOM Type Handling: Differentiates between "production" and "finance" BOMs. It calculates a version 
number (SAPVersion) by adding:
+31 for production BOMs
+1 for finance BOMs
Material Info: Pads the material number to 18 digits (SAP standard), and includes material type and 
description.
Performance Metrics: Includes throughput and crewing quantities from the BOM system.
Filters:
Only active recipes (VALID_END_DATE IS NULL)
Only inventory point indicators (INVN_PT_IND = 1)
Excludes materials with type starting with ZCWP and description starting with PH -
2. sap_data CTE: Extract SAP System Data
This section pulls data from SAP tables to get the most recent and valid routing and BOM information.
Nested CTE: pi05_step
Extracts step -level data for routing steps with control key PI05, which typically represents a standard 
production step.
Filters for:
Valid records (valid_to = '9999 -12-31')
Not deleted (loekz IS NULL)
Routing type 2
Most recent valid -from date (datuv)
Main SAP Data Extraction
Joins routing (mapl), BOM (plmz), and production version (mkal) data.
Pads material numbers and BOM alternatives to match formatting.
Pulls throughput and crewing values from the pi05_step CTE.
Filters for:
Routing type 2
Valid and active records
BOM type M (likely manufacturing)
3. reconciliation CTE: Compare BOM and SAP Data
This section performs the core comparison between the BOM and SAP datasets.
Joins the two datasets on:
Plant code
SAP version (must match both BOM alternative and last 2 digits of production version)
Material number
Compares:
Throughput values (Throughput_BOM vs Throughput_SAP)
Crewing values (Crewing_BOM vs Crewing_SAP)
Tolerance: Uses a threshold of 0.001 to determine if values are considered "matched".
Discrepancy Classification:
'Missing in SAP': No matching SAP record
'Missing in BOM': No matching BOM record
'Throughput', 'Crewing', or 'Throughput, Crewing': Indicates which values differ
'No Discrepancies Exist': Everything matches
4. Final Query: Filter and Deduplicate Discrepancies
This final section filters and deduplicates the results:
Filters out:
Records with no discrepancies
Records missing in either system
Uses ROW_NUMBER() to:
Partition by material number, BOM type, and version
Keep only the first discrepancy per group (based on RecipeID)
Final Output
The result is a clean, deduplicated list of discrepancies between the BOM and SAP systems, showing only 
the first instance of each unique discrepancy per material/version/type combination. This is useful for:
Auditing data consistency
Identifying integration issues
Supporting master data governance

Wednesday, July 09, 2025 10:14 AM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 766    
         --Assign row numbers to group by material, BOM type, and version
         ROW_NUMBER () OVER (
           PARTITION BY Material_Number , BOMType , BOM_Version
           ORDER BY RecipeID
         ) AS rn
    FROM reconciliation
  WHERE Discrepancies != 'No Discrepancies Exist'
    AND Discrepancies != 'Missing in SAP'
    AND Discrepancies != 'Missing in BOM'
)
WHERE rn= 1;

1. Introduction
1.1 Purpose
The purpose of this document is to outline the business requirements for the component level reconciliation 
between SAP and the BOM Portal. This reconciliation aims to ensure data consistency and accuracy in 
throughput, crewing, and component -level data.
1.2 Scope
This BRD covers the requirements for the reconciliation process, including data sources, field mappings, 
composite key logic, and reconciliation logic. It does not cover the implementation details or technical Thursday, July 10, 2025 3:12 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 769    
composite key logic, and reconciliation logic. It does not cover the implementation details or technical 
specifications.
1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, and Abbreviations
SAP: Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing•
BOM: Bill of Materials•
BRD: Business Requirements Document•
PBI: Power BI•
1.4 References
Component Reconciliation Report SAP and BOM Portal.docx•
Component -Level Reconciliation Data.docx •
SAP to BOM Portal Reconciliation Requirement.docx•
2. Business Objectives
2.1 Background
Tyson Foods uses SAP for enterprise resource planning and the BOM Portal for managing bill of materials. 
Discrepancies between these systems can lead to operational inefficiencies and inaccuracies in reporting.
2.2 Business Goals
Ensure data consistency between SAP and BOM Portal.•
Improve accuracy in throughput and crewing data.•
Enhance decision -making through reliable component -level data. •

4. Requirements
4.1 Functional Requirements
FR1: Data Extraction
Description: Develop a process to extract component -level data from SAP and BOM Portal. •
Details: 
Source Systems: SAP, BOM Portal •
Frequency: Daily •
Data Fields: Material ID, Plant, BOM Version, Throughput, Crewing, Component Details ••
FR2: Field Mappings
Description: Implement field mappings between SAP and BOM Portal data. •
Details: 
Mapping Document: Define a mapping document that specifies how fields in SAP correspond to 
fields in the BOM Portal.•
Example Mapping: 
SAP Material ID -> BOM Portal Material ID •
SAP Plant -> BOM Portal Plant •
SAP BOM Version -> BOM Portal BOM Version •••
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 770    
SAP BOM Version -> BOM Portal BOM Version •
FR3: Composite Key Logic
Description: Define composite key logic for identifying unique records. •
Details: 
Composite Key Components: Material ID, Plant, BOM Version •
Example: CONCAT(Material ID, Plant, BOM Version) ••
FR4: Reconciliation Logic
Description: Create reconciliation logic to compare data from both systems. •
Details: 
Comparison Criteria: Match records based on composite key and compare throughput, crewing, 
and component details.•
Discrepancy Identification: Highlight records with mismatched data. ••
FR5: Discrepancy Reporting
Description: Generate discrepancy reports highlighting inconsistencies. •
Details: 
Report Format: CSV, Excel, PDF •
Report Fields: Composite Key, Field Name, SAP Value, BOM Portal Value, Discrepancy Type ••
4.2 Non -Functional Requirements
NFR1: Performance
Description: The reconciliation process should run daily. •
Details: 
Execution Time: Complete within 2 hours •
Data Volume: Handle up to 1 million records per run ••
NFR2: Scalability
Description: The system should handle large volumes of data efficiently. •
Details: 
Scalability Plan: Implement scalable data processing techniques such as parallel processing. ••
NFR3: Security
Description: Ensure data security and compliance with Tyson's data governance policies. •
Details: 
Data Encryption: Encrypt data in transit and at rest. •
Access Control: Implement role -based access control. ••
4.3 Data Requirements

Description: Data sources include SAP tables and BOM Portal databases. •
Details: 
SAP Tables: [List of relevant SAP tables] •
BOM Portal Databases: [List of relevant BOM Portal databases] ••
DR2: Field Mappings
Description: Field mappings should cover throughput, crewing, and component -level data. •
Details: 
Mapping Document: Maintain an up -to-date mapping document. •
Example Fields: 
SAP Throughput -> BOM Portal Throughput •
SAP Crewing -> BOM Portal Crewing •••
DR3: Composite Keys
Description: Composite keys will be based on material ID, plant, and BOM version. •
Details: 
Key Definition: CONCAT(Material ID, Plant, BOM Version) ••
4.4 User Requirements
UR1: Reconciliation Reports
Description: Users should be able to view reconciliation reports via a dashboard. •
Details: 
Dashboard Tool: Power BI ••

5. Assumptions and Constraints
5.1 Assumptions
A1:Data extraction tools are available and functional.•
A2:Stakeholders will provide necessary access to data sources.•
A3:The reconciliation process can be automated.•
5.2 Constraints
C1:Limited by existing data integration capabilities.•
C2:Dependent on the availability of SAP and BOM Portal systems.•
C3:Compliance with Tyson's data governance policies.•
6. Risks
6.1 Risk Identification
R1:Data extraction failures due to system downtime.•
R2:Inaccurate field mappings leading to incorrect reconciliation.•
R3:Performance issues with large data volumes.•
R4:Security breaches compromising sensitive data.•
6.2 Risk Mitigation
RM1: Implement automated alerts for extraction failures.•
RM2: Conduct thorough testing of field mappings before deployment.•
RM3: Optimize data processing techniques for performance.•
RM4: Implement robust security measures including encryption and access control.•
7. Appendices
7.1 Glossary
Reconciliation: The process of ensuring data consistency between two systems. •
Composite Key: A combination of two or more columns used to uniquely identify a record. •
Throughput: The amount of material or items passing through a system or process. •
Crewing: The allocation of personnel to tasks or shifts. •

   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 773    
Purpose
This report ensures that Bill of Materials (BOM) data in the BOM Portal aligns with the official source of 
record in SAP. Accurate alignment between these systems is critical for:
Costing accuracy•
Production planning•
Compliance and audit readiness•
What We Did
Pulled BOM data from two sources : •
BOM Portal : Internal system used for BOM management and costing. •
SAP (CS03) : Corporate ERP system, source of truth for BOM structures. •
Compared data at two levels:•
Primary BOM level : Checks if BOM versions exist in both systems for each material and plant. •
Component level : Validates that all materials inside a BOM match between systems. •
Key Highlights
Excluded inactive or irrelevant BOMs (e.g., placeholder materials).•
Normalized BOM version logic so both systems align for comparison.•
Identified discrepancies where:•
A BOM exists in one system but not the other . •
Components are missing from one system . •
Why It Matters
Eliminates risk of incorrect production or finance reporting due to BOM mismatches.•
Supports faster BOM approvals and reduces manual error -checking . •
Improves transparency for cost variance analysis and inventory accuracy . •
Next Steps
Share discrepancy report with business teams.•
Prioritize resolution of high -impact mismatches (e.g., production -critical BOMs). •
Implement regular reconciliation process to keep systems aligned. •
For Andre: 
Goal
Build a reconciliation framework to identify and track discrepancies between BOM Portal and SAP for 
both header -level BOMs and component -level details .
What the Query DoesComponent Level Explanation
Monday, July 28, 2025 4:45 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 774    
What the Query Does
Extracts BOM Data1.
From BOM Portal: Active Production & Finance BOMs tied to inventory points. •
From SAP (CS03): Valid BOMs (effective & not flagged for deletion). •
Normalizes BOM Versioning2.
Applies logic to match version formats between systems.•
Compares Across Two Dimensions3.
Primary -Level Check: Does a BOM exist for the same material, plant, and version in both systems? •
Component -Level Check: Do all components match for that BOM? •
Outputs4.
Consolidated discrepancy list:•
Primary mismatches (filtered to exclude cases missing entirely from one system).•
Component mismatches with descriptions for BOM alignment.•
Value for Product
Visibility: Provides a data -driven view of where and why BOM data diverges. •
Efficiency: Reduces manual SAP vs. Portal comparison efforts. •
Quality: Drives data consistency for downstream processes like costing and production. •
Key Metrics to Monitor
Number of mismatched BOMs by Plant , Material Type , BOM Type . •
% of component -level discrepancies per BOM type. •
Time to resolution for identified mismatches.•
Next Steps for Product Roadmap

Automate monthly reconciliation runs and link with change management workflows . •
Explore API-based syncs or validations during BOM creation/editing. •
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 775    
Step 1: BOM Portal Data ( bom_data )
sql
CopyEdit
bom_data AS (
  SELECT
    r.RECIPE_ID,
    p.PLANT_CODE,
    m.MATERIAL_NUM,
    m.MATERIAL_DESCR,
    m.MATERIAL_TYPE,
    r.BOM_TYPE,
    CASE
      WHEN r.BOM_TYPE = 'Production' THEN CAST(r.SAP_VERSION_NUM + 31 AS 
STRING)
      WHEN r.BOM_TYPE = 'Finance'    THEN LPAD(CAST(r.SAP_VERSION_NUM + 1 AS 
STRING), 2, '0')
      ELSE r.BOM_TYPE
    END AS BOM_VERSION,
    DATE(r.VALID_START_DATE) AS VALID_START_DATE
  ...
Pulls BOMs from BOM Portal for recipes that: •
Are active ( VALID_END_DATE IS NULL ) •
Are for Production or Finance •
Have at least one inventory point ( rfx.INVN_PT_IND = 1 ) •
Transforms SAP version numbers from BOM Portal into comparable BOM_VERSION format. •
Joins with Plant , Material , and mapping tables to get descriptive fields. •
Step 2: SAP BOM Data ( sap_data )
sql
CopyEdit
sap_data AS (
  SELECT
    mast.WERKS AS PLANT_CODE,
    REGEXP_REPLACE(mast.MATNR, r'^0+', '') AS MATERIAL_NUM,
    makt.MAKTX AS SAP_MATERIAL_DESCR,
    mara.MTART AS MATERIAL_TYPE,
    CASE mast.STLAN
      WHEN '1' THEN 'Production'
      WHEN '6' THEN 'Finance'
    END AS BOM_TYPE,
    mast.STLAL AS BOM_VERSION,
    DATE(stko.DATUV) AS VALID_START_DATE,
    stko.STLNR AS SAP_BOM_NUM
  ...
Pulls BOM header data from SAP tables : •
Filters only valid BOMs ( VALID_TO = '9999 -12-31') •
Includes Plant , Material , Material Description , and BOM Type . •Technical Explanaiton
Monday, July 28, 2025 4:46 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 776    
Includes Plant , Material , Material Description , and BOM Type . •
Removes leading zeros from material numbers for alignment with BOM Portal.•
Step 3: Combine All Unique Keys ( all_keys )
sql
CopyEdit
all_keys AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT PLANT_CODE, MATERIAL_NUM, MATERIAL_TYPE, BOM_TYPE, 
BOM_VERSION FROM bom_data
  UNION DISTINCT
  SELECT DISTINCT PLANT_CODE, MATERIAL_NUM, MATERIAL_TYPE, BOM_TYPE, 
BOM_VERSION FROM sap_data
),
Creates a master list of unique BOM identifiers (Plant, Material, BOM Type, BOM Version) from both 
systems.•
Ensures we can compare apples -to-apples across systems. •
Step 4: Find Primary BOM Discrepancies ( primary_discrepancies )
sql
CopyEdit
primary_discrepancies AS (
  SELECT
    ak.PLANT_CODE,
    ak.MATERIAL_NUM,
    ak.MATERIAL_TYPE,
    ak.BOM_TYPE,
    ak.BOM_VERSION,
    bd.RECIPE_ID,
    bd.MATERIAL_DESCR,
    sd.SAP_MATERIAL_DESCR,
    sd.SAP_BOM_NUM,
    CASE
      WHEN bd.RECIPE_ID IS NULL THEN 'Not on BOM Portal'
      WHEN sd.SAP_BOM_NUM IS NULL THEN 'Not on SAP'
      ELSE 'Match'
    END AS discrepancy
Joins all_keys back to both sources to check: •
Exists in BOM Portal only → Not on SAP •
Exists in SAP only → Not on BOM Portal •
Exists in both → Match •
Step 5: BOM Portal Component Data ( bom_component_data )
Pulls component -level details from BOM Portal: •
Primary Material → BOM Header •
Component Material → Item in BOM •
Filters active BOMs ( VALID_END_DATE IS NULL ) •
Step 6: SAP Component Data ( sap_component_data )
Similar logic but from SAP tables:•
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 777    
Similar logic but from SAP tables:•
Maps routing relationships to components using PLMZ/STPO.•
Step 7: Merge All Components ( all_component_combinations )
Creates a union of all (Plant, Primary Material, BOM Version, Component Material) pairs from both 
systems.•
Ensures full coverage for component comparison.•
Step 8: Component Discrepancies ( component_discrepancies )
sql
CopyEdit
CASE
  WHEN bd.COMPONENT_MATERIAL IS NULL THEN 'Not on BOM Portal'
  WHEN sd.COMPONENT_MATERIAL IS NULL THEN 'Not on SAP'
Identifies mismatched components:•
If exists in SAP only →  ot on BOM Portal •
If exists in BOM Portal only →  ot on SAP •
Step 9: Combine Discrepancies ( final_discrepancies )
sql
CopyEdit
final_discrepancies AS (
  SELECT ... FROM primary_discrepancies WHERE discrepancy NOT IN ('Match', 
'Not on BOM Portal', 'Not on SAP')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ... FROM component_discrepancies WHERE discrepancy <> 'Match'
)
Filters out fully missing BOMs (already known issue). •
Keeps only actual differences that need review. •
Adds placeholder columns for consistent schema between primary and component discrepancies.•
Final SELECT
sql
CopyEdit
SELECT
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY PLANT_CODE, MATERIAL_NUM, BOM_VERSION, 
COMPONENT_MATERIAL) AS row_id,
  *
FROM final_discrepancies
ORDER BY PLANT_CODE, MATERIAL_NUM, BOM_VERSION, COMPONENT_MATERIAL;
Adds a unique row number for reporting.•
Sorts by Plant → Material → Version → Component for readability. •
✅What the Query Accomplishes
Compares BOM structure between SAP and BOM Portal . •
Identifies:•
Primary BOM mismatches → BOM exists in both systems but differs in detail. •
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 778    
Primary BOM mismatches → BOM exists in both systems but differs in detail. •
Component mismatches → Components exist in one system but not the other. •
Produces a clean, combined discrepancy report with descriptive fields for analysis. •

WITH bom_components AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    p.PLANT_CODE,
    m.MATERIAL_NUM AS PRIMARY_MATERIAL,
    cm.MATERIAL_NUM AS COMPONENT_MATERIAL
  FROM `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.recipe
_current` r
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_material_xref_cur
rent` pmx
    ON r.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = pmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_current` p
    ON pmx.PLANT_ID = p.PLANT_ID
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.material_current` m
    ON pmx.MATERIAL_ID = m.MATERIAL_ID
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.recipe_formulation_xref
_current` rfx
    ON r.RECIPE_ID = rfx.RECIPE_ID
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.formul
ation_step_current` fs

  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.step_c
omponent_group_current` scg
    ON scg.FORMULATION_STEP_ID = fs.FORMULATION_STEP_ID
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bompdb_prod_bom_mysql_restricted_confidential.step_c
omponent_current` sc
    ON sc.STEP_COMPONENT_GROUP_ID = scg.STEP_COMPONENT_GROUP_ID
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.plant_material_xref_cur
rent` cpmx
    ON sc.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID = cpmx.PLANT_MATERIAL_ID
  JOIN `prod -2134-
entdatalake -5938ee.mysql_bom_mysql_bom_mysql_internal.material_current` cm
    ON cpmx.MATERIAL_ID = cm.MATERIAL_ID
  WHERE r.VALID_END_DATE IS NULL
    AND r.BOM_TYPE IN ('Production','Finance')
),
sap_components AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    mast.werks AS PLANT_CODE,
    REGEXP_REPLACE(mast.matnr, r'^0+', '') AS PRIMARY_MATERIAL,
    REGEXP_REPLACE(stpo.idnrk, r'^0+', '') AS COMPONENT_MATERIAL
  FROM `prod -2134-entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_internal.mast_current` mast
  JOIN `prod -2134-entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_confidential.stpo_current` stpo
    ON mast.stlnr = stpo.stlnr
  JOIN `prod -2134-entdatalake -5938ee.bigquery_prod_2434_entdataingest_
05104f_s4hana_confidential.stko_current` stko
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 781    
05104f_s4hana_confidential.stko_current` stko
    ON mast.stlnr = stko.stlnr AND mast.stlal = stko.stlal
  WHERE stko.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'
    AND stpo.valid_to = '9999 -12-31'
    AND mast.stlan IN ('1','6') --Production or Finance
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS bom_only_components
FROM bom_components b
LEFT JOIN sap_components s
  ON s.PLANT_CODE = b.PLANT_CODE
AND s.PRIMARY_MATERIAL = b.PRIMARY_MATERIAL
AND s.COMPONENT_MATERIAL = b.COMPONENT_MATERIAL
WHERE s.COMPONENT_MATERIAL IS NULL;

✅3. Analysis of Problems You’re Facing
Here’s what likely falls under your issues list based on previous reconciliation work:
A) Query Logic & Data Alignment Issues
Version Logic•
Portal BOM version uses +31 for Production, +1for Finance → Risk of mismatch if SAP versioning logic 
differs.•
Header vs Component Mapping•
Header -level matches exist, but components differ (missing in SAP or BOM Portal). •
Null Handling & Padding•
LPAD and CASE expressions sometimes cause mismatched string vs numeric types.•
Plant -level Joins•
PLANT_CODE joins may fail when plant_material_xref records are missing. •
B) Data Quality & System Discrepancies
Orphan Records•
Recipes exist in BOM Portal without SAP counterpart (or vice versa).•
Inventory Point Indicator (INV_PT_IND)•
Some BOMs flagged incorrectly → extra rows in result sets. •
Component Usage %, Scrap % Differences•
Values don’t reconcile due to transformation or stale data.•
Throughput & Crewing Mismatch•
Different SAP transaction data vs BOM Portal values due to timing of sync.•
C) Performance & Query Complexity
Large Joins•
Multiple table joins across GCP BigQuery and MySQL → timeouts or slow execution. •
Data Volume•
Millions of rows processed → performance optimization needed (indexes, filtering). •Problems
Thursday, July 31, 2025 3:19 PM
   BOM -SAP Reconciliation Component Level Page 783    
Millions of rows processed → performance optimization needed (indexes, filtering). •
D) Access & Refresh Problems
Restricted Dataset Refresh•
Blocked refresh (your second screenshot issue) → causes stale Power BI visuals. •
Semantic Models Locked•
Models included in App cannot be deleted → making workspace cleanup harder. •
✅4. Root Causes Identified
Version number transformation inconsistencies → mismatches in joins. •
Legacy BOM Portal vs SAP structural differences → missing mappings. •
Gateway or credential restrictions → blocked refresh. •
Dependencies in published app → cannot delete datasets. •

Research Findings: 
Weekly BOM -to-PDM reconciliation report data corresponds to Material Spec number reference. •
Material Spec numbers are derived from the operation option from a Formulation Spec item.•
Confirmation needed from R&D that all materials have a material spec.•
Material spec numbers do not directly relate to SAP (PR3 or S4) Material Master numbers.•
Material spec numbers can only be checked through corresponding trade specs.•
Related specs options attached to a material spec include trade definition and formulation spec.•
SAP4MM can be mapped to the material spec through trade specs.•1.
Process Definition: 
Query data for ZSWP and ZFG Material types.•
Retrieve official trade specs with attributes: 
material_num (SAP4MM reference)•
trade_spec_num•
trade_spec_version•
trade_spec_status (must show "Official")••
Crosscheck related specs: 
Determine if the trade spec contains a lower -level item (Consumer Unit). •
If lower -level item is attached, retrieve attributes: 
[consumer unit] trade_spec_num•
[consumer unit] trade_spec_version•
[consumer unit] trade_spec_status (can be non -official but attached to official main 
trade spec)•••
Check material specifications section from <related specs> tab: 
Obtain material spec reference ("Spec Name").•
Retrieve attributes from material spec: 
material_spec_number•
material_spec_version•
material_spec_status (attached to consumer/trade specification)•••
If no lower -level item is attached, retrieve material spec directly from <related specs> tab. •2.

Based on your interest in actively engaging with the stock market for enjoyment and your current 
portfolio, here's a detailed reallocation strategy focusing on high -growth and speculative investments:
1. Fidelity Go Account
Current Holdings : Diversified funds including Fidelity Flex 500 Index Fund (FDFIX), Fidelity Flex 
International Index Fund (FITFX), Fidelity Flex Mid Cap Index Fund (FLAPX), Fidelity Flex Small Cap Index 
Fund (FLXSX), and Fidelity Flex Municipal Income Fund (FUENX).•
Reallocation Strategy :
Fidelity Flex 500 Index Fund (FDFIX) : Maintain or slightly increase allocation to continue exposure 
to large -cap U.S. equities.•
Fidelity Flex International Index Fund (FITFX) : Consider increasing allocation to capitalize on 
potential growth in international markets.•
Fidelity Flex Mid Cap Index Fund (FLAPX) and Fidelity Flex Small Cap Index Fund (FLXSX) : 
Maintain current allocations to benefit from potential growth in mid and small -cap companies.•
Fidelity Flex Municipal Income Fund (FUENX) : Evaluate the necessity of this holding based on 
your risk tolerance and income needs; consider reallocating funds to higher -growth options if 
appropriate.••
2. Individual Account
Current Holdings : A mix of technology stocks (Apple, Amazon, NVIDIA, Palantir), ETFs (SPY), and other 
equities.•
Reallocation Strategy :
Apple Inc. (AAPL) : Continue holding; the stock has shown consistent performance with a current 
price of $243.35.•
Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) : Maintain position; currently trading at $218.85, indicating strong 
market presence.•
NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) : Hold or consider increasing allocation; trading at $141.92, NVIDIA remains 
a leader in AI and graphics processing.•
Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) : Reassess based on performance; currently at $69.235, with 
recent volatility.•
SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) : Maintain as a core holding for broad market exposure; currently 
priced at $605.57.•
Super Micro Computer Inc. (SMCI) : Evaluate performance; trading at $40.22, consider potential 
growth prospects.•
Exxon Mobil Corp. (XOM) : Assess based on energy market trends; currently at $115.58. ••
3. 401(k) Account
Current Holdings : SS Target Retirement 2065 Fund. •
Reallocation Strategy :
Given the long -term horizon, maintaining the current allocation is appropriate. •
If seeking more aggressive growth, explore available higher -risk funds within the 401(k) plan. ••

significant growth potential.•

Diversification : Ensure a balanced mix of high -growth and speculative stocks to spread risk. •
Regular Monitoring : Keep a close eye on market trends and individual stock performance to make 
informed decisions.•
Risk Management : Be prepared for volatility, especially with speculative investments, and adjust 
allocations as needed.•
Note: Investing in the stock market involves risks, including the potential loss of principal. It's important 
to conduct thorough research or consult with a financial advisor before making investment decisions.

"Casual Connections NWA."•
Group Description:
Clearly outline the purpose of the group in the description. You can use phrases like "for 
singles looking for no -strings -attached connections in a safe and respectful environment."•
Include a disclaimer about respecting boundaries and consent, and that harassment or 
inappropriate behavior will not be tolerated.•
Remind members that while the group is for casual interactions, it should still maintain 
respectful communication and behavior.•
Group Settings:
Privacy : Set the group to "Private" so only members can see the content. •
Membership Approval : Require admin or moderator approval for new members to 
ensure a level of quality and adherence to group guidelines.•
Post Approval : Turn on post approval by admins/moderators to monitor what content 
gets shared in the group.•
Rules & Guidelines:
Set clear rules around consent, respectful behavior, and communication.•
Prohibit sharing of private information (like nudes) publicly in the group to avoid 
potential issues with privacy and legal concerns.•
State that any inappropriate messages outside of agreed boundaries will lead to 
removal from the group.•
Moderation Team:
If possible, recruit trusted moderators to help you manage the group, 
especially as it grows. Moderators can help maintain the vibe of the group 
and ensure that all members follow the rules.•
Content Strategy:
Post icebreakers and discussion topics to keep the group engaging. 
For instance, fun prompts, casual meet -up ideas, or tips on 
maintaining open, honest communication.•
Keep the atmosphere light and playful, while reinforcing the 
importance of respect.•
Compliance with Facebook Guidelines:
Be mindful of Facebook's community standards, especially 
regarding adult content and explicit material. Keep the 
group's activities focused on consent and safe interactions.•1.1.
Starting and managing a group like this takes effort to keep the 
community safe and aligned with its purpose, but with the right balance, it 
can become a place where people feel comfortable exploring casual 
connections.1.1.1.1.Spicy Singles NWA

This isn't THAT kind of singles group; it's a social community designed for singles to connect in a 
relaxed group setting, with no pressure for anything beyond friendship and companionship.
Despite the emphasis on a laid -back atmosphere rather than dating (or perhaps because of it!), 
this approach has successfully led to many successful romantic relationships, reminiscent of how 
our parents met -organically and face -to-face.
The concept is straightforward: dating apps often fall short due to endless swiping, awkward 
one-on-one conversations, or the disappointment of online chemistry not translating offline. 
Our solution? The chance to meet REAL singles that live in Washington or Benton county (no 
out of towners allowed!) at one of our nightly Meet -ups!
Queenie Be is revolutionizing how singles in Northwest Arkansas meet and mingle. It has 
fostered countless friendships and sparked many romantic connections. Will you be next?
This is a place to flirt or get to know ppl in a low stress environment. Empathize with other 
singles, not argue them into believing the way you do about a topic, or bring them down with 
negativity. There is plenty of that out there in the world, this is a positive environment.

to navigate the drawer using screen reader browse mode, especially if you want to review the 
static research data under the tabs.

Indicates a Dividend or Capital Gains Distribution exists.

Creating an AI chatbot to simulate engaging conversations on platforms like Seeking Arrangements 
requires careful planning, particularly because the interactions need to feel authentic and personal to 
maintain interest. Here's a breakdown of how you could approach building such a chatbot:
1. Plan the Chatbot's Capabilities
The chatbot should:
Respond in real -time to messages. •
Generate unique and engaging replies to maintain interest.•
Be able to adapt to different tones or topics of conversation.•
Handle multiple conversations simultaneously.•
Remember key details about the individuals (e.g., their interests, preferences, and previous 
messages) to make the responses feel personalized.•
2. Choose a Platform for Development
You can use the following technologies to build your chatbot:
Natural Language Processing (NLP): Use tools like OpenAI’s GPT or similar large language models. •
Chatbot Frameworks: Consider platforms like:
Dialogflow (Google)•
Microsoft Bot Framework•
Rasa (Open Source)••
Custom Implementation: Host your AI using cloud services like AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud. •
3. Define the Flow and Personality
Tone: Write a detailed personality profile for the chatbot (e.g., playful, flirty, attentive). •
Response Types: Define typical message formats:
Small talk: "How’s your day going, handsome?"•
Compliments: " ou’re so impressive with everything you’ve achieved!"•
Personal questions: "What’s been the highlight of your week?"•
Engaging follow -ups: "That’s amazing! Tell me more about it." ••
Boundaries: Ensure the chatbot doesn’t overpromise or respond inappropriately. •
4. Gather Training Data
Use transcripts of similar conversations (if ethical and permitted) to fine -tune the chatbot. •
Incorporate pre -trained models with conversational datasets. •
5. Build the Chatbot
Language Model Fine -Tuning:Use OpenAI’s API or alternatives like Cohere to fine -tune responses 
for your specific purpose.•
Conversation Memory: Implement a database (e.g., MongoDB, Firebase) to store details about 
each user. This helps the bot remember personal details, maintaining the illusion of intimacy.•
Real -Time Messaging Integration: Use APIs to integrate the chatbot with messaging platforms (if •AI Chat Bot for ALI
Friday, January 24, 2025 4:32 PM
   Personal Page 849    
Real -Time Messaging Integration: Use APIs to integrate the chatbot with messaging platforms (if 
applicable).•
6. Monitor and Improve
Set up a feedback loop to monitor the chatbot’s responses and tweak the AI model based on real -
world interactions.•
Implement moderation or escalation for responses needing human intervention.•
7. Compliance and Ethics
Platforms like Seeking Arrangements have terms of service that may prohibit the use of automation 
tools or bots. Ensure:
You have explicit permission to use a chatbot on the platform.•
The chatbot complies with any regulations or guidelines (such as disclosing it’s not human, if 
required).•
Sample Tools for Development
Programming Language: Python (with libraries like transformers and chatterbot ). •
APIs: Twilio, Telegram API, or custom REST APIs for real -time messaging. •
Hosting: Use cloud services for scalability. •
